attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

{ TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID: String(userId) } : {}),\\n      ...entry.env,\\n    },\\n    acpModel: entry.model,\\n    acpEffort: entry.effort,\\n    permissionMode: entry.permissionMode,\\n    promptRetries: 2,\\n    promptRetryBaseMs: 1000,\\n  };\\n\\n  const client = new AcpClient(opts);\\n  client.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n    process.stderr.write(`[specialist:${instanceId}] ${chunk}`);\\n  });\\n  client.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] process exited (code=${code})`);\\n    parallelInstances.delete(instanceId);\\n  });\\n\\n  // If harness uses --agent flag, kiro-cli loads MCP servers from its own\\n  // agent config — passing them again in session/new causes a crash.\\n  const mcpServers = harnessHasAgentFlag(entry) ? Without this, session/new rejects with -32602.\\n */\\nfunction toAcpMcpServers(servers: any[] | undefined): any[] | undefined {\\n  if (!Array.isArray(servers)) return undefined;\\n  const toArr = (env: any) =>\\n    Array.isArray(env)\\n      ? env\\n      : Object.entries(env ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value: String(value) }));\\n  return servers\\n    .filter((s) => s && !s.disabled)\\n    .map((s) => {\\n      if (s.type === \\\"http\\\" || s.type === \\\"sse\\\") {\\n        return {\\n          type: s.type,\\n          name: s.name,\\n          url: s.url,\\n          headers: Array.isArray(s.headers)\\n            ? s.headers\\n            : Object.entries(s.headers ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value: String(value) })),\\n        };\\n      }\\n      return {\\n        name: s.name,\\n        command: s.command,\\n        args: Array.isArray(s.args) ? s.args : [],\\n        env: toArr(s.env),\\n      };\\n    });\\n}\\n\\n/** Expand ${MEMORY_DIR} / ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR} / ${BRIDGE_DIR} in specialist config strings. */\\nfunction expandVars(entry: SpecialistEntry): SpecialistEntry {\\n  const vars: Record<string, string> = {\\n    MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n    AGENT_CONFIG_DIR: loadedAgentConfigDir,\\n    BRIDGE_DIR: path.resolve(config.sessionCwd),\\n  };\\n  const replace = (s: string) =>\\n    s.replace(/\\\\$\\\\{(\\\\w+)\\\\}/g, (_, k) => vars[k] ?? `\\\\${${k}}`);\\n  return {\\n    ...entry,\\n    cwd: replace(entry.cwd),\\n    harness: { command: entry.harness.command, args: entry.harness.args.map(replace) },\\n    env: entry.env\\n      ? Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(entry.env).map(([k, v]) => [k, replace(v)]))\\n      : undefined,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/** Load specialists.json from MEMORY_DIR/config (fallback: AGENT_CONFIG_DIR). Returns count loaded. */\\nexport async function loadSpecialistsConfig(agentConfigDir: string): Promise<number> {\\n  loadedAgentConfigDir = agentConfigDir;\\n  configPath = resolveBridgeConfigPath(\\\"specialists.json\\\", agentConfigDir);\\n  try {\\n    const raw = await readFile(configPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);\\n    if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) {\\n      console.warn(`[specialist] ${configPath} is not an array, ignoring`);\\n      loadedConfig = [];\\n      return 0;\\n    }\\n    loadedConfig = (parsed as SpecialistsConfig).map(expandVars);\\n    console.log(`[specialist] loaded ${loadedConfig.length} specialist(s) from ${configPath}`);\\n    return loadedConfig.length;\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    if (err.code === \\\"ENOENT\\\") {\\n      console.log(`[specialist] no config at ${configPath} — feature disabled`);\\n    } else {\\n      console.warn(`[specialist] failed to load ${configPath}:`, err.message);\\n    }\\n    loadedConfig = [];\\n    return 0;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Transcript persistence ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst transcriptDir = path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"sessions\\\");\\n\\nasync function appendTranscript(name: string, role: \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\", text: string): Promise<void> {\\n  const date = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);\\n  const file = path.join(transcriptDir, `specialist-${name}-${date}.md`);\\n  await mkdir(transcriptDir, { recursive: true });\\n  const prefix = role === \\\"user\\\" ? \\\"**User:**\\\" : `**${name}:**`;\\n  const entry = `${prefix}\\\\n${text}\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n`;\\n  await appendFile(file, entry, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Instance management ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\ninterface LiveInstance {\\n  client: AcpClient;\\n  busy: boolean;\\n  sessionId: string;\\n}\\n\\nconst instances = new Map<string, LiveInstance>();\\n\\nfunction getEntry(name: string): SpecialistEntry | undefined {\\n  return loadedConfig.find((s) => s.name === name);\\n}\\n\\n/** Close and remove a specialist instance. */\\nexport function closeInstance(name: string): void {\\n  const inst = instances.get(name);\\n  if (inst) {\\n    inst.client.close();\\n    instances.delete(name);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] closed (task done)`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Close a parallel instance by its unique instanceId. */\\nexport function closeParallelInstance(instanceId: string): void {\\n  const inst = parallelInstances.get(instanceId);\\n  if (inst) {\\n    inst.client.close();\\n    parallelInstances.delete(instanceId);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] parallel instance closed`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Parallel instances — keyed by unique instanceId, not reused. */\\nconst parallelInstances = new Map<string, LiveInstance>();\\n\\n/** Spawn a fresh instance (no reuse). Returns a unique instanceId. */\\nexport async function spawnNew(name: string, userId?: number): Promise<{ instanceId: string; inst: LiveInstance }> {\\n  const entry = getEntry(name);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown specialist: ${name}`);\\n  return spawnEphemeral(entry, userId);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Spawn a fresh instance from an explicit entry (not looked up in\\n * specialists.json). Used by MoA references (src/moa.ts), which build their\\n * entries from specialist-domains.json at call time. Tracked in\\n * parallelInstances under a unique instanceId — close with closeParallelInstance.\\n */\\nexport async function spawnEphemeral(entry: SpecialistEntry, userId?: number): Promise<{ instanceId: string; inst: LiveInstance }> {\\n  const name = entry.name;\\n  const instanceId = `${name}-${Math.random().toString(16).slice(2, 6)}`;\\n\\n  const opts: AcpClientOptions = {\\n    command: entry.harness.command,\\n    args: [...entry.harness.args],\\n    cwd: path.resolve(entry.cwd),\\n    // TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID rides the same process-env path as MEMORY_DIR — the ACP\\n    // adapter forwards parent env to its MCP stdio children, so the memory server\\n    // resolves the active user id (else list_facts fails \\\"active user id not configured\\\").\\n    env: {\\n      MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n      ...(userId ? { TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID: String(userId) } : {}),\\n      ...entry.env,\\n    },\\n    acpModel: entry.model,\\n    acpEffort: entry.effort,\\n    permissionMode: entry.permissionMode,\\n    promptRetries: 2,\\n    promptRetryBaseMs: 1000,\\n  };\\n\\n  const client = new AcpClient(opts);\\n  client.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n    process.stderr.write(`[specialist:${instanceId}] ${chunk}`);\\n  });\\n  client.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] process exited (code=${code})`);\\n    parallelInstances.delete(instanceId);\\n  });\\n\\n  // If harness uses --agent flag, kiro-cli loads MCP servers from its own\\n  // agent config — passing them again in session/new causes a crash.\\n  const mcpServers = harnessHasAgentFlag(entry) ? undefined : toAcpMcpServers(entry.mcpServers);\\n  const sessionId = await client.initializeAndCreateSession(opts.cwd, mcpServers);\\n  const inst: LiveInstance = { client, busy: false, sessionId };\\n  parallelInstances.set(instanceId, inst);\\n  console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] spawned (parallel), session=${sessionId}`);\\n  logEvent(\\\"specialist_spawn\\\", { instanceId, sessionId, parallel: true });\\n  return { instanceId, inst };\\n}\\n\\nasync function spawnOrReuse(name: string, userId?: number): Promise<LiveInstance> {\\n  const existing = instances.get(name);\\n  if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) return existing;\\n\\n  // Clean up stale entry\\n  if (existing) instances.delete(name);\\n\\n  const entry = getEntry(name);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown specialist: ${name}`);\\n\\n  const opts: AcpClientOptions = {\\n    command: entry.harness.command,\\n    args: [...entry.harness.args],\\n    cwd: path.resolve(entry.cwd),\\n    // See spawnNew: TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID rides the same process-env path as MEMORY_DIR\\n    // so the memory MCP child can resolve the active user id.\\n    env: {\\n      MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n      ...(userId ? { TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID: String(userId) } : {}),\\n      ...entry.env,\\n    },\\n    acpModel: entry.model,\\n    acpEffort: entry.effort,\\n    promptRetries: 2,\\n    promptRetryBaseMs: 1000,\\n  };\\n\\n  const client = new AcpClient(opts);\\n  client.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n    process.stderr.write(`[specialist:${name}] ${chunk}`);\\n  });\\n  client.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] process exited (code=${code})`);\\n    instances.delete(name);\\n  });\\n\\n  const sessionId = await client.initializeAndCreateSession(opts.cwd,\\n    harnessHasAgentFlag(entry) ? undefined : toAcpMcpServers(entry.mcpServers));\\n  const inst: LiveInstance = { client, busy: false, sessionId };\\n  instances.set(name, inst);\\n  console.log(`[specialist:${name}] spawned, session=${sessionId}`);\\n  logEvent(\\\"specialist_spawn\\\", { name, sessionId, parallel: false });\\n  return inst;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Dispatch ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface DispatchResult {\\n  payload: ResultPayload;\\n  /** Raw text chunks collected from the specialist's response. */\\n  rawOutput: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Dispatch a delegation to a local specialist.\\n * Spawns/reuses the specialist, sends the goal prompt, collects the response,\\n * and returns a structured ResultPayload.\\n */\\nexport async function dispatchToSpecialist(\\n  target: string,\\n  delegate: DelegatePayload,\\n  userId?: number,\\n): Promise<DispatchResult> {\\n  const entry = getEntry(target);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown: ${target}`);\\n\\n  const inst = await spawnOrReuse(target, userId);\\n  if (inst.busy) {\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: \\\"Specialist is busy with another task\\\" },\\n      rawOutput: \\\"\\\",\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  inst.busy = true;\\n  const maxTurns = delegate.maxTurns ?? entry.maxTurns ?? 15;\\n  const timeoutMs = entry.timeoutMs ?? 1_800_000;\\n\\n  // Register with status store\\n  registerTask({\\n    taskId: delegate.id,\\n    type: \\\"specialist\\\",\\n    specialist: target,\\n    goal: delegate.goal.slice(0, 100),\\n  });\\n\\n  // Build preamble from relevant topic shards\\n  const preamble = await buildSpecialistPreamble(target, userId ?? 0).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  // Run scoped enrichment based on the delegation goal\\n  const scope = await getSpecialistScope(target).catch(() => undefined);\\n  const enrichment = await runEnrichment(delegate.goal, userId ?? 0, undefined, scope).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  // Build the goal prompt\\n  const prompt = buildGoalPrompt(delegate, preamble, enrichment);\\n\\n  let output = \\\"\\\";\\n  const updateHandler = (u: SessionUpdate) => {\\n    const up = u.update;\\n    if (\\n      (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" || up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\") \\n    ) {\\n      const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n      if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n        output += content.text;\\n        const role = up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\" ? \\\"thought\\\" : \\\"agent\\\";\\n        broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"chunk\\\", role, text: content.text });\\n      }\\n    }\\n  };\\n  inst.client.on(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n\\n  try {\\n    // Multi-turn loop: keep prompting until agent says done or budget exhausted\\n    let turns = 0;\\n    let lastStopReason = \\\"\\\";\\n\\n    const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\\n\\n    while (turns < maxTurns && Date.now() < deadline) {\\n      turns++;\\n      updateTask(delegate.id, { turns, lastOutput: output.slice(-200) || undefined });\\n      broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status: \\\"running\\\", turns });\\n      const promptText = turns === 1 ? prompt : \\\"[系統] 繼續執行，完成後在回覆中說明結果。\\\";\\n\\n      const result = await Promise.race([\\n        inst.client.prompt(promptText),\\n        new Promise<never>((_, reject) =>\\n          setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(\\\"timeout\\\")), Math.max(1000, deadline - Date.now())),\\n        ),\\n      ]);\\n\\n      lastStopReason = result.stopReason;\\n      // If agent stopped normally (end_turn), check if it needs more turns\\n      if (lastStopReason === \\\"end_turn\\\" || lastStopReason === \\\"stop\\\") break;\\n      // tool_use means agent is still working — loop for next turn\\n    }\\n\\n    // Extract summary from output (last 2000 chars as summary)\\n    const summary = output.length > 2000 ? output.slice(-2000) : output || \\\"(no output)\\\";\\n\\n    const status: ResultPayload[\\\"status\\\"] =\\n      Date.now() >= deadline ? \\\"timeout\\\" : \\\"done\\\";\\n\\n    completeTask(delegate.id, status, { lastOutput: summary.slice(0, 200) });\\n    broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status });\\n\\n    // Persist transcript for /memorytoskill\\n    appendTranscript(target, \\\"user\\\", prompt).catch(() => {});\\n    appendTranscript(target, \\\"assistant\\\", output).catch(() => {});\\n\\n    logEvent(\\\"specialist_done\\\", { target, taskId: delegate.id, status, turns });\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status, summary },\\n      rawOutput: output,\\n    };\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    const msg = err.message ?? String(err);\\n    const failStatus = msg.includes(\\\"timeout\\\") ? \\\"timeout\\\" : \\\"failed\\\";\\n    completeTask(delegate.id, failStatus, { error: msg.slice(0, 200) });\\n    broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status: failStatus });\\n    logEvent(\\\"specialist_done\\\", { target, taskId: delegate.id, status: failStatus, error: msg.slice(0, 100) });\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: `Error: ${msg.slice(0, 500)}` },\\n      rawOutput: output,\\n    };\\n  } finally {\\n    inst.client.removeListener(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n    inst.busy = false;\\n    closeInstance(target);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction buildGoalPrompt(d: DelegatePayload, preamble: string, enrichment?: string): string {\\n  let prompt = preamble;\\n  if (enrichment) prompt += enrichment;\\n  prompt += `[Delegation Task — id: ${d.id}]\\\\n`;\\n  prompt += `Goal: ${d.goal}\\\\n`;\\n  if (d.context) prompt += `Context: ${d.context}\\\\n`;\\n  prompt += `\\\\nComplete this task. When done, summarize what you accomplished.`;\\n  return prompt;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Parallel Dispatch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ParallelTask {\\n  taskId: string;\\n  target: string;\\n  payload: DelegatePayload;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ParallelResult {\\n  taskId: string;\\n  target: string;\\n  status: \\\"done\\\" | \\\"timeout\\\" | \\\"failed\\\";\\n  summary: string;\\n  rawOutput: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Dispatch multiple delegations in parallel. Each gets its own fresh instance.\\n * Returns after all settle (Promise.allSettled).\\n */\\nexport async function dispatchParallel(\\n  tasks: ParallelTask[],\\n  userId?: number,\\n): Promise<ParallelResult[]> {\\n  const promises = tasks.map(async (task): Promise<ParallelResult> => {\\n    const entry = getEntry(task.target);\\n    if (!entry) {\\n      return { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: `Unknown specialist: ${task.target}`, rawOutput: \\\"\\\" };\\n    }\\n\\n    let instanceId = \\\"\\\";\\n    try {\\n      registerTask({\\n        taskId: task.taskId,\\n        type: \\\"parallel_delegate\\\",\\n        specialist: task.target,\\n        goal: task.payload.goal.slice(0, 100),\\n      });\\n      const spawned = await spawnNew(task.target, userId);\\n      instanceId = spawned.instanceId;\\n      const inst = spawned.inst;\\n\\n      const maxTurns = task.payload.maxTurns ?? entry.maxTurns ?? 15;\\n      const timeoutMs = entry.timeoutMs ?? 1_800_000;\\n\\n      const preamble = await buildSpecialistPreamble(task.target, userId ?? 0).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n      const scope = await getSpecialistScope(task.target).catch(() => undefined);\\n      const enrichment = await runEnrichment(task.payload.goal, userId ?? 0, undefined, scope).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n      const prompt = buildGoalPrompt(task.payload, preamble, enrichment);\\n\\n      let output = \\\"\\\";\\n      const updateHandler = (u: SessionUpdate) => {\\n        const up = u.update;\\n        if (\\n          (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" || up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\")\\n        ) {\\n          const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n          if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n            output += content.text;\\n            const role = up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\" ? \\\"thought\\\" : \\\"agent\\\";\\n            broadcastSSE(task.taskId, { type: \\\"chunk\\\", role, text: content.text });\\n          }\\n        }\\n      };\\n      inst.client.on(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n\\n      try {\\n        let turns = 0;\\n        const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\\n\\n        while (turns < maxTurns && Date.now() < deadline) {\\n          turns++;\\n          const promptText = turns === 1 ? prompt : \\\"[系統] 繼續執行，完成後在回覆中說明結果。\\\";\\n          const result = await Promise.race([\\n            inst.client.prompt(promptText),\\n            new Promise<never>((_, reject) =>\\n              setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(\\\"timeout\\\")), Math.max(1000, deadline - Date.now())),\\n            ),\\n          ]);\\n          if (result.stopReason === \\\"end_turn\\\" || result.stopReason === \\\"stop\\\") break;\\n        }\\n\\n        const summary = output.length > 2000 ? output.slice(-2000) : output || \\\"(no output)\\\";\\n        const status: ParallelResult[\\\"status\\\"] = Date.now() >= deadline ? \\\"timeout\\\" : \\\"done\\\";\\n\\n        // Verifier gate (same as RELAY_DELEGATE path)\\n        if (status === \\\"done\\\" && output.length > 50) {\\n          const vr = await verifySpecialistOutput(task.payload.goal, output);\\n          logEvent(\\\"specialist_done\\\", { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, verifier: vr.pass ? \\\"PASS\\\" : \\\"NEEDS_FIX\\\", source: vr.source, issues: vr.issues?.slice(0, 300) });\\n          if (!vr.pass && vr.issues) {\\n            console.log(`[parallel:${task.taskId}] verifier NEEDS_FIX (${vr.source}): ${vr.issues.slice(0, 100)}`);\\n            // Retry once\\n            try {\\n              const retryPrompt = buildRetryPrompt(task.payload.goal, vr.issues);\\n              output = \\\"\\\";\\n              const retryResult = await Promise.race([\\n                inst.client.prompt(retryPrompt),\\n                new Promise<never>((_, reject) => setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(\\\"timeout\\\")), 120_000)),\\n              ]);\\n              void retryResult;\\n            } catch { /* retry failed — use original output */ }\\n          }\\n        }\\n\\n        completeTask(task.taskId, status, { lastOutput: summary.slice(0, 200) });\\n\\n        appendTranscript(task.target, \\\"user\\\", prompt).catch(() => {});\\n        appendTranscript(task.target, \\\"assistant\\\", output).catch(() => {});\\n\\n        return { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, status, summary, rawOutput: output };\\n      } finally {\\n        inst.client.removeListener(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n        closeParallelInstance(instanceId);\\n      }\\n    } catch (err: any) {\\n      if (instanceId) closeParallelInstance(instanceId);\\n      const msg = (err.message ?? String(err)).slice(0, 500);\\n      completeTask(task.taskId, \\\"failed\\\", { error: msg.slice(0, 200) });\\n      return { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: `Error: ${msg}`, rawOutput: \\\"\\\" };\\n    }\\n  });\\n\\n  const settled = await Promise.allSettled(promises);\\n  return settled.map((s, i) =>\\n    s.status === \\\"fulfilled\\\"\\n      ? s.value\\n      : { taskId: tasks[i].taskId, target: tasks[i].target, status: \\\"failed\\\" as const, summary: `Unexpected: ${s.reason}`, rawOutput: \\\"\\\" },\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Cleanup ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport async function shutdownAllSpecialists(): Promise<void> {\\n  for (const [name, inst] of instances) {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] shutting down...`);\\n    await inst.client.close();\\n  }\\n  instances.clear();\\n  proxyState.clear();\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Transparent Proxy Mode ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst PROXY_DONE_RE = /<<PROXY_DONE>>/i;\\n\\ninterface ProxySession {\\n  specialistName: string;\\n  /** Conversation history for end-of-proxy summary. */\\n  history: { role: \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\"; text: string }[];\\n  startedAt: number;\\n  lastActivity: number;\\n  /** Facts already injected via enrichment — dedup across turns. */\\n  injectedFacts: Set<string>;\\n  /** Total chars sent to specialist (preamble + enrichment + user text). */\\n  charsIn: number;\\n  /** Total chars received from specialist. */\\n  charsOut: number;\\n}\\n\\nconst proxyState = new Map<number, ProxySession>();\\n\\n/** Check if a chatId is currently in proxy mode. */\\nexport function isProxyActive(chatId: number): boolean {\\n  return proxyState.has(chatId);\\n}\\n\\n/** Get the active proxy specialist name for a chatId. */\\nexport function getProxyTarget(chatId: number): string | undefined {\\n  return proxyState.get(chatId)?.specialistName;\\n}\\n\\n/** Get cumulative char stats for the active proxy session. */\\nexport function getProxyStats(chatId: number): { charsIn: number; charsOut: number } | undefined {\\n  const ps = proxyState.get(chatId);\\n  return ps ? { charsIn: ps.charsIn, charsOut: ps.charsOut } : undefined;\\n}\\n\\n/** Enter proxy mode: subsequent user messages go directly to this specialist. */\\nexport function startProxy(chatId: number, specialistName: string): void {\\n  proxyState.set(chatId, {\\n    specialistName,\\n    history: [],\\n    startedAt: Date.now(),\\n    lastActivity: Date.now(),\\n    injectedFacts: new Set(),\\n    charsIn: 0,\\n    charsOut: 0,\\n  });\\n  console.log(`[specialist:proxy] chat ${chatId} → proxy to \\\"${specialistName}\\\"`);\\n}\\n\\n/** Exit proxy mode. Returns the conversation history for summary injection. */\\nexport function endProxy(chatId: number): ProxySession | undefined {\\n  const session = proxyState.get(chatId);\\n  proxyState.delete(chatId);\\n  if (session) {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] chat ${chatId} exited proxy (${session.history.length} exchanges)`);\\n    closeInstance(session.specialistName);\\n  }\\n  return session;\\n}\\n\\n/** Register a callback for idle timeout auto-exit. */\\n/**\\n * Send a user message to the proxied specialist and collect the response.\\n * Returns the raw output text (caller is responsible for sending to Telegram\\n * and extracting memory tokens).\\n */\\nexport async function promptProxy(\\n  chatId: number,\\n  text: string,\\n  onChunk?: (currentOutput: string) => void,\\n  userId?: number,\\n): Promise<{ output: string; stopReason: string; proxyDone: boolean }> {\\n  const ps = proxyState.get(chatId);\\n  if (!ps) throw new Error(\\\"[specialist:proxy] no active proxy for this chat\\\");\\n\\n  const inst = await spawnOrReuse(ps.specialistName, userId);\\n\\n  // First prompt: prepend preamble + instruction so specialist knows about PROXY_DONE\\n  const isFirst = ps.history.length === 0;\\n  let prompt: string;\\n  if (isFirst) {\\n    const preamble = await buildSpecialistPreamble(ps.specialistName, userId ?? 0, { proxy: true }).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n    // Run scoped enrichment for the first message too\\n    const scope = await getSpecialistScope(ps.specialistName).catch(() => undefined);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] enrichment scope for ${ps.specialistName}:`, JSON.stringify(scope ?? null));\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] subsystem ready: embed=${isEmbedModelReady()}, wiki=${isWikiRetrievalReady()}, recall=${isMemoryRecallReady()}`);\\n    const enrichment = await runEnrichment(text, userId ?? 0, ps.injectedFacts, scope).catch((e) => { console.warn(\\\"[specialist:proxy] enrichment error:\\\", e); return \\\"\\\"; });\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] enrichment result length: ${enrichment.length}${enrichment.length > 0 ? \\\" (has content)\\\" : \\\" (empty)\\\"}`);\\n    prompt = `${preamble}[System] You are in a proxy conversation with the user. Do NOT append <<PROXY_DONE>> unless the user explicitly ends the conversation (says goodbye, \\\"沒了\\\", \\\"就這樣\\\", \\\"thanks that's all\\\", etc.). null));\\n    const enrichment = await runEnrichment(text, userId ?? 0, ps.injectedFacts, scope).catch((e) => { console.warn(\\\"[specialist:proxy] turn enrichment error:\\\", e); return \\\"\\\"; });\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] turn enrichment length: ${enrichment.length}`);\\n    prompt = enrichment ? Returns count loaded. */\\nexport async function loadSpecialistsConfig(agentConfigDir: string): Promise<number> {\\n  loadedAgentConfigDir = agentConfigDir;\\n  configPath = resolveBridgeConfigPath(\\\"specialists.json\\\", agentConfigDir);\\n  try {\\n    const raw = await readFile(configPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);\\n    if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) {\\n      console.warn(`[specialist] ${configPath} is not an array, ignoring`);\\n      loadedConfig = [];\\n      return 0;\\n    }\\n    loadedConfig = (parsed as SpecialistsConfig).map(expandVars);\\n    console.log(`[specialist] loaded ${loadedConfig.length} specialist(s) from ${configPath}`);\\n    return loadedConfig.length;\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    if (err.code === \\\"ENOENT\\\") {\\n      console.log(`[specialist] no config at ${configPath} — feature disabled`);\\n    } else {\\n      console.warn(`[specialist] failed to load ${configPath}:`, err.message);\\n    }\\n    loadedConfig = [];\\n    return 0;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Transcript persistence ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst transcriptDir = path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"sessions\\\");\\n\\nasync function appendTranscript(name: string, role: \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\", text: string): Promise<void> {\\n  const date = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);\\n  const file = path.join(transcriptDir, `specialist-${name}-${date}.md`);\\n  await mkdir(transcriptDir, { recursive: true });\\n  const prefix = role === \\\"user\\\" ? \\\"**User:**\\\" : `**${name}:**`;\\n  const entry = `${prefix}\\\\n${text}\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n`;\\n  await appendFile(file, entry, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Instance management ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\ninterface LiveInstance {\\n  client: AcpClient;\\n  busy: boolean;\\n  sessionId: string;\\n}\\n\\nconst instances = new Map<string, LiveInstance>();\\n\\nfunction getEntry(name: string): SpecialistEntry | undefined {\\n  return loadedConfig.find((s) => s.name === name);\\n}\\n\\n/** Close and remove a specialist instance. */\\nexport function closeInstance(name: string): void {\\n  const inst = instances.get(name);\\n  if (inst) {\\n    inst.client.close();\\n    instances.delete(name);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] closed (task done)`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Close a parallel instance by its unique instanceId. */\\nexport function closeParallelInstance(instanceId: string): void {\\n  const inst = parallelInstances.get(instanceId);\\n  if (inst) {\\n    inst.client.close();\\n    parallelInstances.delete(instanceId);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] parallel instance closed`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Parallel instances — keyed by unique instanceId, not reused. */\\nconst parallelInstances = new Map<string, LiveInstance>();\\n\\n/** Spawn a fresh instance (no reuse). Returns a unique instanceId. */\\nexport async function spawnNew(name: string, userId?: number): Promise<{ instanceId: string; inst: LiveInstance }> {\\n  const entry = getEntry(name);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown specialist: ${name}`);\\n  return spawnEphemeral(entry, userId);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Spawn a fresh instance from an explicit entry (not looked up in\\n * specialists.json). Used by MoA references (src/moa.ts), which build their\\n * entries from specialist-domains.json at call time. Tracked in\\n * parallelInstances under a unique instanceId — close with closeParallelInstance.\\n */\\nexport async function spawnEphemeral(entry: SpecialistEntry, userId?: number): Promise<{ instanceId: string; inst: LiveInstance }> {\\n  const name = entry.name;\\n  const instanceId = `${name}-${Math.random().toString(16).slice(2, 6)}`;\\n\\n  const opts: AcpClientOptions = {\\n    command: entry.harness.command,\\n    args: [...entry.harness.args],\\n    cwd: path.resolve(entry.cwd),\\n    // TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID rides the same process-env path as MEMORY_DIR — the ACP\\n    // adapter forwards parent env to its MCP stdio children, so the memory server\\n    // resolves the active user id (else list_facts fails \\\"active user id not configured\\\").\\n    env: {\\n      MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n      ...(userId ? { TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID: String(userId) } : {}),\\n      ...entry.env,\\n    },\\n    acpModel: entry.model,\\n    acpEffort: entry.effort,\\n    permissionMode: entry.permissionMode,\\n    promptRetries: 2,\\n    promptRetryBaseMs: 1000,\\n  };\\n\\n  const client = new AcpClient(opts);\\n  client.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n    process.stderr.write(`[specialist:${instanceId}] ${chunk}`);\\n  });\\n  client.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] process exited (code=${code})`);\\n    parallelInstances.delete(instanceId);\\n  });\\n\\n  // If harness uses --agent flag, kiro-cli loads MCP servers from its own\\n  // agent config — passing them again in session/new causes a crash.\\n  const mcpServers = harnessHasAgentFlag(entry) ? undefined : toAcpMcpServers(entry.mcpServers);\\n  const sessionId = await client.initializeAndCreateSession(opts.cwd, mcpServers);\\n  const inst: LiveInstance = { client, busy: false, sessionId };\\n  parallelInstances.set(instanceId, inst);\\n  console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] spawned (parallel), session=${sessionId}`);\\n  logEvent(\\\"specialist_spawn\\\", { instanceId, sessionId, parallel: true });\\n  return { instanceId, inst };\\n}\\n\\nasync function spawnOrReuse(name: string, userId?: number): Promise<LiveInstance> {\\n  const existing = instances.get(name);\\n  if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) return existing;\\n\\n  // Clean up stale entry\\n  if (existing) instances.delete(name);\\n\\n  const entry = getEntry(name);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown specialist: ${name}`);\\n\\n  const opts: AcpClientOptions = {\\n    command: entry.harness.command,\\n    args: [...entry.harness.args],\\n    cwd: path.resolve(entry.cwd),\\n    // See spawnNew: TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID rides the same process-env path as MEMORY_DIR\\n    // so the memory MCP child can resolve the active user id.\\n    env: {\\n      MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n      ...(userId ? { TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID: String(userId) } : {}),\\n      ...entry.env,\\n    },\\n    acpModel: entry.model,\\n    acpEffort: entry.effort,\\n    promptRetries: 2,\\n    promptRetryBaseMs: 1000,\\n  };\\n\\n  const client = new AcpClient(opts);\\n  client.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n    process.stderr.write(`[specialist:${name}] ${chunk}`);\\n  });\\n  client.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] process exited (code=${code})`);\\n    instances.delete(name);\\n  });\\n\\n  const sessionId = await client.initializeAndCreateSession(opts.cwd,\\n    harnessHasAgentFlag(entry) ? undefined : toAcpMcpServers(entry.mcpServers));\\n  const inst: LiveInstance = { client, busy: false, sessionId };\\n  instances.set(name, inst);\\n  console.log(`[specialist:${name}] spawned, session=${sessionId}`);\\n  logEvent(\\\"specialist_spawn\\\", { name, sessionId, parallel: false });\\n  return inst;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Dispatch ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface DispatchResult {\\n  payload: ResultPayload;\\n  /** Raw text chunks collected from the specialist's response. */\\n  rawOutput: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Dispatch a delegation to a local specialist.\\n * Spawns/reuses the specialist, sends the goal prompt, collects the response,\\n * and returns a structured ResultPayload.\\n */\\nexport async function dispatchToSpecialist(\\n  target: string,\\n  delegate: DelegatePayload,\\n  userId?: number,\\n): Promise<DispatchResult> {\\n  const entry = getEntry(target);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown: ${target}`);\\n\\n  const inst = await spawnOrReuse(target, userId);\\n  if (inst.busy) {\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: \\\"Specialist is busy with another task\\\" },\\n      rawOutput: \\\"\\\",\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  inst.busy = true;\\n  const maxTurns = delegate.maxTurns ?? entry.maxTurns ?? 15;\\n  const timeoutMs = entry.timeoutMs ?? 1_800_000;\\n\\n  // Register with status store\\n  registerTask({\\n    taskId: delegate.id,\\n    type: \\\"specialist\\\",\\n    specialist: target,\\n    goal: delegate.goal.slice(0, 100),\\n  });\\n\\n  // Build preamble from relevant topic shards\\n  const preamble = await buildSpecialistPreamble(target, userId ?? 0).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  // Run scoped enrichment based on the delegation goal\\n  const scope = await getSpecialistScope(target).catch(() => undefined);\\n  const enrichment = await runEnrichment(delegate.goal, userId ?? 0, undefined, scope).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  // Build the goal prompt\\n  const prompt = buildGoalPrompt(delegate, preamble, enrichment);\\n\\n  let output = \\\"\\\";\\n  const updateHandler = (u: SessionUpdate) => {\\n    const up = u.update;\\n    if (\\n      (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" || up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\") \\n    ) {\\n      const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n      if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n        output += content.text;\\n        const role = up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\" ? \\\"thought\\\" : \\\"agent\\\";\\n        broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"chunk\\\", role, text: content.text });\\n      }\\n    }\\n  };\\n  inst.client.on(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n\\n  try {\\n    // Multi-turn loop: keep prompting until agent says done or budget exhausted\\n    let turns = 0;\\n    let lastStopReason = \\\"\\\";\\n\\n    const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\\n\\n    while (turns < maxTurns && Date.now() < deadline) {\\n      turns++;\\n      updateTask(delegate.id, { turns, lastOutput: output.slice(-200) || undefined });\\n      broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status: \\\"running\\\", turns });\\n      const promptText = turns === 1 ? prompt : \\\"[系統] 繼續執行，完成後在回覆中說明結果。\\\";\\n\\n      const result = await Promise.race([\\n        inst.client.prompt(promptText),\\n        new Promise<never>((_, reject) =>\\n          setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(\\\"timeout\\\")), Math.max(1000, deadline - Date.now())),\\n        ),\\n      ]);\\n\\n      lastStopReason = result.stopReason;\\n      // If agent stopped normally (end_turn), check if it needs more turns\\n      if (lastStopReason === \\\"end_turn\\\" || lastStopReason === \\\"stop\\\") break;\\n      // tool_use means agent is still working — loop for next turn\\n    }\\n\\n    // Extract summary from output (last 2000 chars as summary)\\n    const summary = output.length > 2000 ? output.slice(-2000) : output || \\\"(no output)\\\";\\n\\n    const status: ResultPayload[\\\"status\\\"] =\\n      Date.now() >= deadline ? \\\"timeout\\\" : \\\"done\\\";\\n\\n    completeTask(delegate.id, status, { lastOutput: summary.slice(0, 200) });\\n    broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status });\\n\\n    // Persist transcript for /memorytoskill\\n    appendTranscript(target, \\\"user\\\", prompt).catch(() => {});\\n    appendTranscript(target, \\\"assistant\\\", output).catch(() => {});\\n\\n    logEvent(\\\"specialist_done\\\", { target, taskId: delegate.id, status, turns });\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status, summary },\\n      rawOutput: output,\\n    };\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    const msg = err.message ?? String(err);\\n    const failStatus = msg.includes(\\\"timeout\\\") ? \\\"timeout\\\" : \\\"failed\\\";\\n    completeTask(delegate.id, failStatus, { error: msg.slice(0, 200) });\\n    broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status: failStatus });\\n    logEvent(\\\"specialist_done\\\", { target, taskId: delegate.id, status: failStatus, error: msg.slice(0, 100) });\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: `Error: ${msg.slice(0, 500)}` },\\n      rawOutput: output,\\n    };\\n  } finally {\\n    inst.client.removeListener(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n    inst.busy = false;\\n    closeInstance(target);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction buildGoalPrompt(d: DelegatePayload, preamble: string, enrichment?: string): string {\\n  let prompt = preamble;\\n  if (enrichment) prompt += enrichment;\\n  prompt += `[Delegation Task — id: ${d.id}]\\\\n`;\\n  prompt += `Goal: ${d.goal}\\\\n`;\\n  if (d.context) prompt += `Context: ${d.context}\\\\n`;\\n  prompt += `\\\\nComplete this task. When done, summarize what you accomplished.`;\\n  return prompt;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Parallel Dispatch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ParallelTask {\\n  taskId: string;\\n  target: string;\\n  payload: DelegatePayload;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ParallelResult {\\n  taskId: string;\\n  target: string;\\n  status: \\\"done\\\" | \\\"timeout\\\" | \\\"failed\\\";\\n  summary: string;\\n  rawOutput: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Dispatch multiple delegations in parallel. Each gets its own fresh instance.\\n * Returns after all settle (Promise.allSettled).\\n */\\nexport async function dispatchParallel(\\n  tasks: ParallelTask[],\\n  userId?: number,\\n): Promise<ParallelResult[]> {\\n  const promises = tasks.map(async (task): Promise<ParallelResult> => {\\n    const entry = getEntry(task.target);\\n    if (!entry) {\\n      return { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: `Unknown specialist: ${task.target}`, rawOutput: \\\"\\\" };\\n    }\\n\\n    let instanceId = \\\"\\\";\\n    try {\\n      registerTask({\\n        taskId: task.taskId,\\n        type: \\\"parallel_delegate\\\",\\n        specialist: task.target,\\n        goal: task.payload.goal.slice(0, 100),\\n      });\\n      const spawned = await spawnNew(task.target, userId);\\n      instanceId = spawned.instanceId;\\n      const inst = spawned.inst;\\n\\n      const maxTurns = task.payload.maxTurns ?? entry.maxTurns ?? 15;\\n      const timeoutMs = entry.timeoutMs ?? 1_800_000;\\n\\n      const preamble = await buildSpecialistPreamble(task.target, userId ?? 0).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n      const scope = await getSpecialistScope(task.target).catch(() => undefined);\\n      const enrichment = await runEnrichment(task.payload.goal, userId ?? 0, undefined, scope).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n      const prompt = buildGoalPrompt(task.payload, preamble, enrichment);\\n\\n      let output = \\\"\\\";\\n      const updateHandler = (u: SessionUpdate) => {\\n        const up = u.update;\\n        if (\\n          (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" || up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\")\\n        ) {\\n          const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n          if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n            output += content.text;\\n            const role = up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\" ? \\\"thought\\\" : \\\"agent\\\";\\n            broadcastSSE(task.taskId, { type: \\\"chunk\\\", role, text: content.text });\\n          }\\n        }\\n      };\\n      inst.client.on(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n\\n      try {\\n        let turns = 0;\\n        const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\\n\\n        while (turns < maxTurns && Date.now() < deadline) {\\n          turns++;\\n          const promptText = turns === 1 ? prompt : \\\"[系統] 繼續執行，完成後在回覆中說明結果。\\\";\\n          const result = await Promise.race([\\n            inst.client.prompt(promptText),\\n            new Promise<never>((_, reject) =>\\n              setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(\\\"timeout\\\")), Math.max(1000, deadline - Date.now())),\\n            ),\\n          ]);\\n          if (result.stopReason === \\\"end_turn\\\" || result.stopReason === \\\"stop\\\") break;\\n        }\\n\\n        const status: ParallelResult[\\\"status\\\"] = Date.now() >= deadline ? \\\"timeout\\\" : \\\"done\\\";\\n\\n        // Verifier gate (same as RELAY_DELEGATE path)\\n        if (status === \\\"done\\\" && output.length > 50) {\\n          const vr = await verifySpecialistOutput(task.payload.goal, output);\\n          logEvent(\\\"specialist_done\\\", { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, verifier: vr.pass ? \\\"PASS\\\" : \\\"NEEDS_FIX\\\", source: vr.source, issues: vr.issues?.slice(0, 300) });\\n          if (!vr.pass && vr.issues) {\\n            console.log(`[parallel:${task.taskId}] verifier NEEDS_FIX (${vr.source}): ${vr.issues.slice(0, 100)}`);\\n            // Retry once\\n            try {\\n              const retryPrompt = buildRetryPrompt(task.payload.goal, vr.issues);\\n              output = \\\"\\\";\\n              const retryResult = await Promise.race([\\n                inst.client.prompt(retryPrompt),\\n                new Promise<never>((_, reject) => setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(\\\"timeout\\\")), 120_000)),\\n              ]);\\n              void retryResult;\\n            } catch { /* retry failed — use original output */ }\\n          }\\n        }\\n\\n        completeTask(task.taskId, status, { lastOutput: summary.slice(0, 200) });\\n\\n        appendTranscript(task.target, \\\"user\\\", prompt).catch(() => {});\\n        appendTranscript(task.target, \\\"assistant\\\", output).catch(() => {});\\n\\n        return { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, status, summary, rawOutput: output };\\n      } finally {\\n        inst.client.removeListener(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n        closeParallelInstance(instanceId);\\n      }\\n    } catch (err: any) {\\n      if (instanceId) closeParallelInstance(instanceId);\\n      const msg = (err.message ?? String(err)).slice(0, 500);\\n      completeTask(task.taskId, \\\"failed\\\", { error: msg.slice(0, 200) });\\n      return { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: `Error: ${msg}`, rawOutput: \\\"\\\" };\\n    }\\n  });\\n\\n  const settled = await Promise.allSettled(promises);\\n  return settled.map((s, i) =>\\n    s.status === \\\"fulfilled\\\"\\n      ? s.value\\n      : { taskId: tasks[i].taskId, target: tasks[i].target, status: \\\"failed\\\" as const, summary: `Unexpected: ${s.reason}`, rawOutput: \\\"\\\" },\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Cleanup ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport async function shutdownAllSpecialists(): Promise<void> {\\n  for (const [name, inst] of instances) {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] shutting down...`);\\n    await inst.client.close();\\n  }\\n  instances.clear();\\n  proxyState.clear();\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Transparent Proxy Mode ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst PROXY_DONE_RE = /<<PROXY_DONE>>/i;\\n\\ninterface ProxySession {\\n  specialistName: string;\\n  /** Conversation history for end-of-proxy summary. */\\n  history: { role: \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\"; text: string }[];\\n  startedAt: number;\\n  lastActivity: number;\\n  /** Facts already injected via enrichment — dedup across turns. */\\n  injectedFacts: Set<string>;\\n  /** Total chars sent to specialist (preamble + enrichment + user text). */\\n  charsIn: number;\\n  /** Total chars received from specialist. */\\n  charsOut: number;\\n}\\n\\nconst proxyState = new Map<number, ProxySession>();\\n\\n/** Check if a chatId is currently in proxy mode. */\\nexport function isProxyActive(chatId: number): boolean {\\n  return proxyState.has(chatId);\\n}\\n\\n/** Get the active proxy specialist name for a chatId. */\\nexport function getProxyTarget(chatId: number): string | undefined {\\n  return proxyState.get(chatId)?.specialistName;\\n}\\n\\n/** Get cumulative char stats for the active proxy session. */\\nexport function getProxyStats(chatId: number): { charsIn: number; charsOut: number } | undefined {\\n  const ps = proxyState.get(chatId);\\n  return ps ? { charsIn: ps.charsIn, charsOut: ps.charsOut } : undefined;\\n}\\n\\n/** Enter proxy mode: subsequent user messages go directly to this specialist. */\\nexport function startProxy(chatId: number, specialistName: string): void {\\n  proxyState.set(chatId, {\\n    specialistName,\\n    history: [],\\n    startedAt: Date.now(),\\n    lastActivity: Date.now(),\\n    injectedFacts: new Set(),\\n    charsIn: 0,\\n    charsOut: 0,\\n  });\\n  console.log(`[specialist:proxy] chat ${chatId} → proxy to \\\"${specialistName}\\\"`);\\n}\\n\\n/** Exit proxy mode. Returns the conversation history for summary injection. */\\nexport function endProxy(chatId: number): ProxySession | undefined {\\n  const session = proxyState.get(chatId);\\n  proxyState.delete(chatId);\\n  if (session) {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] chat ${chatId} exited proxy (${session.history.length} exchanges)`);\\n    closeInstance(session.specialistName);\\n  }\\n  return session;\\n}\\n\\n/** Register a callback for idle timeout auto-exit. */\\n/**\\n * Send a user message to the proxied specialist and collect the response.\\n * Returns the raw output text (caller is responsible for sending to Telegram\\n * and extracting memory tokens).\\n */\\nexport async function promptProxy(\\n  chatId: number,\\n  text: string,\\n  onChunk?: (currentOutput: string) => void,\\n  userId?: number,\\n): Promise<{ output: string; stopReason: string; proxyDone: boolean }> {\\n  const ps = proxyState.get(chatId);\\n  if (!ps) throw new Error(\\\"[specialist:proxy] no active proxy for this chat\\\");\\n\\n  const inst = await spawnOrReuse(ps.specialistName, userId);\\n\\n  // First prompt: prepend preamble + instruction so specialist knows about PROXY_DONE\\n  const isFirst = ps.history.length === 0;\\n  let prompt: string;\\n  if (isFirst) {\\n    const preamble = await buildSpecialistPreamble(ps.specialistName, userId ?? 0, { proxy: true }).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n    // Run scoped enrichment for the first message too\\n    const scope = await getSpecialistScope(ps.specialistName).catch(() => undefined);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] enrichment scope for ${ps.specialistName}:`, JSON.stringify(scope ?? null));\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] subsystem ready: embed=${isEmbedModelReady()}, wiki=${isWikiRetrievalReady()}, recall=${isMemoryRecallReady()}`);\\n    const enrichment = await runEnrichment(text, userId ?? 0, ps.injectedFacts, scope).catch((e) => { console.warn(\\\"[specialist:proxy] enrichment error:\\\", e); return \\\"\\\"; });\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] enrichment result length: ${enrichment.length}${enrichment.length > 0 ? \\\" (has content)\\\" : \\\" (empty)\\\"}`);\\n    prompt = `${preamble}[System] You are in a proxy conversation with the user. Do NOT append <<PROXY_DONE>> unless the user explicitly ends the conversation (says goodbye, \\\"沒了\\\", \\\"就這樣\\\", \\\"thanks that's all\\\", etc.). Simply answering a question does NOT mean the conversation is over — always assume follow-ups.\\\\n\\\\n${enrichment}User: ${text}`;\\n  } else {\\n    // Subsequent turns: still run scoped enrichment (dynamic per-turn)\\n    const scope = await getSpecialistScope(ps.specialistName).catch(() => undefined);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] turn enrichment scope:`, JSON.stringify(scope ?? null));\\n    const enrichment = await runEnrichment(text, userId ?? 0, ps.injectedFacts, scope).catch((e) => { console.warn(\\\"[specialist:proxy] turn enrichment error:\\\", e); return \\\"\\\"; });\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] turn enrichment length: ${enrichment.length}`);\\n    prompt = enrichment ? `${enrichment}${text}` : text;\\n  }\\n\\n  let output = \\\"\\\";\\n  const updateHandler = (u: SessionUpdate) => {\\n    const up = u.update;\\n    if (\\n      (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" || up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\")\\n    ) {\\n      const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n      if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n        output += content.text;\\n        onChunk?.(output);\\n      }\\n    }\\n  };\\n  inst.client.on(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n\\n  try {\\n    const result = await inst.client.prompt(prompt);\\n    ps.charsIn += prompt.length;\\n    ps.charsOut += output.length;\\n    ps.history.push({ role: \\\"user\\\", text });\\n    ps.history.push({ role: \\\"assistant\\\", text: output });\\n    ps.lastActivity = Date.now();\\n    // Persist transcript for /memorytoskill\\n    appendTranscript(ps.specialistName, \\\"user\\\", text).catch(() => {});\\n    appendTranscript(ps.specialistName, \\\"assistant\\\", output).catch(() => {});\\n    // Detect <<PROXY_DONE>> — specialist signals it's finished\\n    const proxyDone = PROXY_DONE_RE.test(output);\\n    const cleanOutput = output.replace(PROXY_DONE_RE, \\\"\\\").trim();\\n    return { output: cleanOutput, stopReason: result.stopReason, proxyDone };\\n  } finally {\\n    inst.client.removeListener(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Build a summary of the proxy conversation for injection into main agent. */\\nexport function buildProxySummary(session: ProxySession): string {\\n  const lines: string[] = [];\\n  const count = Math.floor(session.history.length / 2);\\n  // Keep last 5 exchanges max for summary\\n  const recent = session.history.slice(-10);\\n  for (const h of recent) {\\n    const prefix = h.role === \\\"user\\\" ? Kiro/Codex pin model via harness args instead.\\n   */\\n  model?: string;\\n  /** Effort pin (Claude adapter only). Applied after model — see AcpClient. */\\n  effort?: string;\\n  /** Permission policy for agent-initiated requests — see AcpClientOptions. */\\n  permissionMode?: \\\"grant-all\\\" | \\\"readonly\\\";\\n  /** Max turns before force-stopping a delegation. Default 15. */\\n  maxTurns?: number;\\n  /** Timeout (ms) for the entire delegation. Default 1800000 (30min). */\\n  timeoutMs?: number;\\n}\\n\\nexport type SpecialistsConfig = SpecialistEntry[];\\n\\n// ─── Config loading ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nlet loadedConfig: SpecialistsConfig = [];\\nlet configPath = \\\"\\\";\\nlet loadedAgentConfigDir = \\\"\\\";\\n\\nexport function getSpecialists(): SpecialistsConfig {\\n  return loadedConfig;\\n}\\n\\n/** Check if a target name matches a local specialist. */\\nexport function isLocalSpecialist(target: string): boolean {\\n  return loadedConfig.some((s) => s.name === target);\\n}\\n\\n/** Check if harness uses --agent flag (kiro-cli loads MCP servers from agent config). */\\nfunction harnessHasAgentFlag(entry: SpecialistEntry): boolean {\\n  return entry.harness.args.includes(\\\"--agent\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Normalize stored MCP server configs into the ACP `session/new` schema.\\n * The ACP SDK validates each server against zMcpServer: the stdio variant\\n * requires `args` and `env` to be present, and `env` must be an ARRAY of\\n * {name, value} — not the `{KEY: val}` object shape we store. HTTP/SSE\\n * servers keep their `type` and get headers coerced to array form. Disabled\\n * servers are dropped. Without this, session/new rejects with -32602.\\n */\\nfunction toAcpMcpServers(servers: any[] | undefined): any[] | undefined {\\n  if (!Array.isArray(servers)) return undefined;\\n  const toArr = (env: any) =>\\n    Array.isArray(env)\\n      ? env\\n      : Object.entries(env ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value: String(value) }));\\n  return servers\\n    .filter((s) => s && !s.disabled)\\n    .map((s) => {\\n      if (s.type === \\\"http\\\" || s.type === \\\"sse\\\") {\\n        return {\\n          type: s.type,\\n          name: s.name,\\n          url: s.url,\\n          headers: Array.isArray(s.headers)\\n            ? s.headers\\n            : Object.entries(s.headers ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value: String(value) })),\\n        };\\n      }\\n      return {\\n        name: s.name,\\n        command: s.command,\\n        args: Array.isArray(s.args) ? s.args : [],\\n        env: toArr(s.env),\\n      };\\n    });\\n}\\n\\n/** Expand ${MEMORY_DIR} / ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR} / ${BRIDGE_DIR} in specialist config strings. */\\nfunction expandVars(entry: SpecialistEntry): SpecialistEntry {\\n  const vars: Record<string, string> = {\\n    MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n    AGENT_CONFIG_DIR: loadedAgentConfigDir,\\n    BRIDGE_DIR: path.resolve(config.sessionCwd),\\n  };\\n  const replace = (s: string) =>\\n    s.replace(/\\\\$\\\\{(\\\\w+)\\\\}/g, (_, k) => vars[k] ?? `\\\\${${k}}`);\\n  return {\\n    ...entry,\\n    cwd: replace(entry.cwd),\\n    harness: { command: entry.harness.command, args: entry.harness.args.map(replace) },\\n    env: entry.env\\n      ? Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(entry.env).map(([k, v]) => [k, replace(v)]))\\n      : undefined,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/** Load specialists.json from MEMORY_DIR/config (fallback: AGENT_CONFIG_DIR). Returns count loaded. */\\nexport async function loadSpecialistsConfig(agentConfigDir: string): Promise<number> {\\n  loadedAgentConfigDir = agentConfigDir;\\n  configPath = resolveBridgeConfigPath(\\\"specialists.json\\\", agentConfigDir);\\n  try {\\n    const raw = await readFile(configPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);\\n    if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) {\\n      console.warn(`[specialist] ${configPath} is not an array, ignoring`);\\n      loadedConfig = [];\\n      return 0;\\n    }\\n    loadedConfig = (parsed as SpecialistsConfig).map(expandVars);\\n    console.log(`[specialist] loaded ${loadedConfig.length} specialist(s) from ${configPath}`);\\n    return loadedConfig.length;\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    if (err.code === \\\"ENOENT\\\") {\\n      console.log(`[specialist] no config at ${configPath} — feature disabled`);\\n    } else {\\n      console.warn(`[specialist] failed to load ${configPath}:`, err.message);\\n    }\\n    loadedConfig = [];\\n    return 0;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Transcript persistence ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst transcriptDir = path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"sessions\\\");\\n\\nasync function appendTranscript(name: string, role: \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\", text: string): Promise<void> {\\n  const date = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);\\n  const file = path.join(transcriptDir, `specialist-${name}-${date}.md`);\\n  await mkdir(transcriptDir, { recursive: true });\\n  const prefix = role === \\\"user\\\" ? \\\"**User:**\\\" : `**${name}:**`;\\n  const entry = `${prefix}\\\\n${text}\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n`;\\n  await appendFile(file, entry, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Instance management ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\ninterface LiveInstance {\\n  client: AcpClient;\\n  busy: boolean;\\n  sessionId: string;\\n}\\n\\nconst instances = new Map<string, LiveInstance>();\\n\\nfunction getEntry(name: string): SpecialistEntry | undefined {\\n  return loadedConfig.find((s) => s.name === name);\\n}\\n\\n/** Close and remove a specialist instance. */\\nexport function closeInstance(name: string): void {\\n  const inst = instances.get(name);\\n  if (inst) {\\n    inst.client.close();\\n    instances.delete(name);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] closed (task done)`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Close a parallel instance by its unique instanceId. */\\nexport function closeParallelInstance(instanceId: string): void {\\n  const inst = parallelInstances.get(instanceId);\\n  if (inst) {\\n    inst.client.close();\\n    parallelInstances.delete(instanceId);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] parallel instance closed`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Parallel instances — keyed by unique instanceId, not reused. */\\nconst parallelInstances = new Map<string, LiveInstance>();\\n\\n/** Spawn a fresh instance (no reuse). Returns a unique instanceId. */\\nexport async function spawnNew(name: string, userId?: number): Promise<{ instanceId: string; inst: LiveInstance }> {\\n  const entry = getEntry(name);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown specialist: ${name}`);\\n  return spawnEphemeral(entry, userId);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Spawn a fresh instance from an explicit entry (not looked up in\\n * specialists.json). Used by MoA references (src/moa.ts), which build their\\n * entries from specialist-domains.json at call time. Tracked in\\n * parallelInstances under a unique instanceId — close with closeParallelInstance.\\n */\\nexport async function spawnEphemeral(entry: SpecialistEntry, userId?: number): Promise<{ instanceId: string; inst: LiveInstance }> {\\n  const name = entry.name;\\n  const instanceId = `${name}-${Math.random().toString(16).slice(2, 6)}`;\\n\\n  const opts: AcpClientOptions = {\\n    command: entry.harness.command,\\n    args: [...entry.harness.args],\\n    cwd: path.resolve(entry.cwd),\\n    // TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID rides the same process-env path as MEMORY_DIR — the ACP\\n    // adapter forwards parent env to its MCP stdio children, so the memory server\\n    // resolves the active user id (else list_facts fails \\\"active user id not configured\\\").\\n    env: {\\n      MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n      ...(userId ? { TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID: String(userId) } : {}),\\n      ...entry.env,\\n    },\\n    acpModel: entry.model,\\n    acpEffort: entry.effort,\\n    permissionMode: entry.permissionMode,\\n    promptRetries: 2,\\n    promptRetryBaseMs: 1000,\\n  };\\n\\n  const client = new AcpClient(opts);\\n  client.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n    process.stderr.write(`[specialist:${instanceId}] ${chunk}`);\\n  });\\n  client.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] process exited (code=${code})`);\\n    parallelInstances.delete(instanceId);\\n  });\\n\\n  // If harness uses --agent flag, kiro-cli loads MCP servers from its own\\n  // agent config — passing them again in session/new causes a crash.\\n  const mcpServers = harnessHasAgentFlag(entry) ? undefined : toAcpMcpServers(entry.mcpServers);\\n  const sessionId = await client.initializeAndCreateSession(opts.cwd, mcpServers);\\n  const inst: LiveInstance = { client, busy: false, sessionId };\\n  parallelInstances.set(instanceId, inst);\\n  console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] spawned (parallel), session=${sessionId}`);\\n  logEvent(\\\"specialist_spawn\\\", { instanceId, sessionId, parallel: true });\\n  return { instanceId, inst };\\n}\\n\\nasync function spawnOrReuse(name: string, userId?: number): Promise<LiveInstance> {\\n  const existing = instances.get(name);\\n  if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) return existing;\\n\\n  // Clean up stale entry\\n  if (existing) instances.delete(name);\\n\\n  const entry = getEntry(name);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown specialist: ${name}`);\\n\\n  const opts: AcpClientOptions = {\\n    command: entry.harness.command,\\n    args: [...entry.harness.args],\\n    cwd: path.resolve(entry.cwd),\\n    // See spawnNew: TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID rides the same process-env path as MEMORY_DIR\\n    // so the memory MCP child can resolve the active user id.\\n    env: {\\n      MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n      ...(userId ? { TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID: String(userId) } : {}),\\n      ...entry.env,\\n    },\\n    acpModel: entry.model,\\n    acpEffort: entry.effort,\\n    promptRetries: 2,\\n    promptRetryBaseMs: 1000,\\n  };\\n\\n  const client = new AcpClient(opts);\\n  client.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n    process.stderr.write(`[specialist:${name}] ${chunk}`);\\n  });\\n  client.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] process exited (code=${code})`);\\n    instances.delete(name);\\n  });\\n\\n  const sessionId = await client.initializeAndCreateSession(opts.cwd,\\n    harnessHasAgentFlag(entry) ? undefined : toAcpMcpServers(entry.mcpServers));\\n  const inst: LiveInstance = { client, busy: false, sessionId };\\n  instances.set(name, inst);\\n  console.log(`[specialist:${name}] spawned, session=${sessionId}`);\\n  logEvent(\\\"specialist_spawn\\\", { name, sessionId, parallel: false });\\n  return inst;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Dispatch ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface DispatchResult {\\n  payload: ResultPayload;\\n  /** Raw text chunks collected from the specialist's response. */\\n  rawOutput: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Dispatch a delegation to a local specialist.\\n * Spawns/reuses the specialist, sends the goal prompt, collects the response,\\n * and returns a structured ResultPayload.\\n */\\nexport async function dispatchToSpecialist(\\n  target: string,\\n  delegate: DelegatePayload,\\n  userId?: number,\\n): Promise<DispatchResult> {\\n  const entry = getEntry(target);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown: ${target}`);\\n\\n  const inst = await spawnOrReuse(target, userId);\\n  if (inst.busy) {\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: \\\"Specialist is busy with another task\\\" },\\n      rawOutput: \\\"\\\",\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  inst.busy = true;\\n  const maxTurns = delegate.maxTurns ?? entry.maxTurns ?? 15;\\n  const timeoutMs = entry.timeoutMs ?? 1_800_000;\\n\\n  // Register with status store\\n  registerTask({\\n    taskId: delegate.id,\\n    type: \\\"specialist\\\",\\n    specialist: target,\\n    goal: delegate.goal.slice(0, 100),\\n  });\\n\\n  // Build preamble from relevant topic shards\\n  const preamble = await buildSpecialistPreamble(target, userId ?? 0).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  // Run scoped enrichment based on the delegation goal\\n  const scope = await getSpecialistScope(target).catch(() => undefined);\\n  const enrichment = await runEnrichment(delegate.goal, userId ?? 0, undefined, scope).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  // Build the goal prompt\\n  const prompt = buildGoalPrompt(delegate, preamble, enrichment);\\n\\n  let output = \\\"\\\";\\n  const updateHandler = (u: SessionUpdate) => {\\n    const up = u.update;\\n    if (\\n      (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" || up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\") \\n    ) {\\n      const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n      if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n        output += content.text;\\n        const role = up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\" ? \\\"thought\\\" : \\\"agent\\\";\\n        broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"chunk\\\", role, text: content.text });\\n      }\\n    }\\n  };\\n  inst.client.on(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n\\n  try {\\n    // Multi-turn loop: keep prompting until agent says done or budget exhausted\\n    let turns = 0;\\n    let lastStopReason = \\\"\\\";\\n\\n    const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\\n\\n    while (turns < maxTurns && Date.now() < deadline) {\\n      turns++;\\n      updateTask(delegate.id, { turns, lastOutput: output.slice(-200) || undefined });\\n      broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status: \\\"running\\\", turns });\\n      const promptText = turns === 1 ? prompt : \\\"[系統] 繼續執行，完成後在回覆中說明結果。\\\";\\n\\n      const result = await Promise.race([\\n        inst.client.prompt(promptText),\\n        new Promise<never>((_, reject) =>\\n          setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(\\\"timeout\\\")), Math.max(1000, deadline - Date.now())),\\n        ),\\n      ]);\\n\\n      lastStopReason = result.stopReason;\\n      // If agent stopped normally (end_turn), check if it needs more turns\\n      if (lastStopReason === \\\"end_turn\\\" || lastStopReason === \\\"stop\\\") break;\\n      // tool_use means agent is still working — loop for next turn\\n    }\\n\\n    // Extract summary from output (last 2000 chars as summary)\\n    const summary = output.length > 2000 ? output.slice(-2000) : output || \\\"(no output)\\\";\\n\\n    const status: ResultPayload[\\\"status\\\"] =\\n      Date.now() >= deadline ? \\\"timeout\\\" : \\\"done\\\";\\n\\n    completeTask(delegate.id, status, { lastOutput: summary.slice(0, 200) });\\n    broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status });\\n\\n    // Persist transcript for /memorytoskill\\n    appendTranscript(target, \\\"user\\\", prompt).catch(() => {});\\n    appendTranscript(target, \\\"assistant\\\", output).catch(() => {});\\n\\n    logEvent(\\\"specialist_done\\\", { target, taskId: delegate.id, status, turns });\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status, summary },\\n      rawOutput: output,\\n    };\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    const msg = err.message ?? content.thinking:
This is a code fix being applied. The primary session is fixing a bug where the summary was calculated before the verifier retry, so if the verifier failed and triggered a retry, the summary would contain the old (rejected) output instead of the retry output.

The fix:
1. Removed the premature `summary` calculation (before verifier)
2. Added it back AFTER the verifier retry block
3. Added a comment explaining why: retry clears output and re-collects, so summary must be calculated after that

This is a bugfix that addresses the issue documented in the handoff as medium #7: "specialist verifier retry 後 summary 沒重算"

Let me create an observation for this.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed specialist verifier retry summary recalculation</title>
  <subtitle>Summary now calculated after verifier retry to match final output instead of rejected first attempt</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>src/specialist.ts dispatchParallel function previously calculated summary before verifier retry block</fact>
    <fact>Verifier retry clears output variable and re-collects specialist response, making early summary stale</fact>
    <fact>Summary calculation moved to after verifier retry block so it captures retry output when verifier rejects first attempt</fact>
    <fact>This ensures summary matches rawOutput when verifier triggers a retry</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed a bug in parallel specialist dispatch where the summary was calculated before the verifier retry logic. When the verifier rejected output and triggered a retry, the code would clear the output variable and re-collect the specialist's retry response. However, since summary was calculated early, it would contain the rejected first-attempt output rather than the successful retry output, creating a mismatch with rawOutput. The fix moves summary calculation to after the verifier block, ensuring it always reflects the final output whether that's the first attempt or a retry.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/specialist.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/specialist.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

Default 1800000 (30min). */\\n  timeoutMs?: number;\\n}\\n\\nexport type SpecialistsConfig = SpecialistEntry[];\\n\\n// ─── Config loading ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nlet loadedConfig: SpecialistsConfig = [];\\nlet configPath = \\\"\\\";\\nlet loadedAgentConfigDir = \\\"\\\";\\n\\nexport function getSpecialists(): SpecialistsConfig {\\n  return loadedConfig;\\n}\\n\\n/** Check if a target name matches a local specialist. */\\nexport function isLocalSpecialist(target: string): boolean {\\n  return loadedConfig.some((s) => s.name === target);\\n}\\n\\n/** Check if harness uses --agent flag (kiro-cli loads MCP servers from agent config). */\\nfunction harnessHasAgentFlag(entry: SpecialistEntry): boolean {\\n  return entry.harness.args.includes(\\\"--agent\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Normalize stored MCP server configs into the ACP `session/new` schema.\\n * The ACP SDK validates each server against zMcpServer: the stdio variant\\n * requires `args` and `env` to be present, and `env` must be an ARRAY of\\n * {name, value} — not the `{KEY: val}` object shape we store.
