Without this, session/new rejects with -32602.\\n */\\nfunction toAcpMcpServers(servers: any[] | undefined): any[] | undefined {\\n  if (!Array.isArray(servers)) return undefined;\\n  const toArr = (env: any) =>\\n    Array.isArray(env)\\n      ? env\\n      : Object.entries(env ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value: String(value) }));\\n  return servers\\n    .filter((s) => s && !s.disabled)\\n    .map((s) => {\\n      if (s.type === \\\"http\\\" || s.type === \\\"sse\\\") {\\n        return {\\n          type: s.type,\\n          name: s.name,\\n          url: s.url,\\n          headers: Array.isArray(s.headers)\\n            ? s.headers\\n            : Object.entries(s.headers ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value: String(value) })),\\n        };\\n      }\\n      return {\\n        name: s.name,\\n        command: s.command,\\n        args: Array.isArray(s.args) ? s.args : [],\\n        env: toArr(s.env),\\n      };\\n    });\\n}\\n\\n/** Expand ${MEMORY_DIR} / ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR} / ${BRIDGE_DIR} in specialist config strings. */\\nfunction expandVars(entry: SpecialistEntry): SpecialistEntry {\\n  const vars: Record<string, string> = {\\n    MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n    AGENT_CONFIG_DIR: loadedAgentConfigDir,\\n    BRIDGE_DIR: path.resolve(config.sessionCwd),\\n  };\\n  const replace = (s: string) =>\\n    s.replace(/\\\\$\\\\{(\\\\w+)\\\\}/g, (_, k) => vars[k] ?? `\\\\${${k}}`);\\n  return {\\n    ...entry,\\n    cwd: replace(entry.cwd),\\n    harness: { command: entry.harness.command, args: entry.harness.args.map(replace) },\\n    env: entry.env\\n      ? Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(entry.env).map(([k, v]) => [k, replace(v)]))\\n      : undefined,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/** Load specialists.json from MEMORY_DIR/config (fallback: AGENT_CONFIG_DIR). Returns count loaded. */\\nexport async function loadSpecialistsConfig(agentConfigDir: string): Promise<number> {\\n  loadedAgentConfigDir = agentConfigDir;\\n  configPath = resolveBridgeConfigPath(\\\"specialists.json\\\", agentConfigDir);\\n  try {\\n    const raw = await readFile(configPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);\\n    if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) {\\n      console.warn(`[specialist] ${configPath} is not an array, ignoring`);\\n      loadedConfig = [];\\n      return 0;\\n    }\\n    loadedConfig = (parsed as SpecialistsConfig).map(expandVars);\\n    console.log(`[specialist] loaded ${loadedConfig.length} specialist(s) from ${configPath}`);\\n    return loadedConfig.length;\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    if (err.code === \\\"ENOENT\\\") {\\n      console.log(`[specialist] no config at ${configPath} — feature disabled`);\\n    } else {\\n      console.warn(`[specialist] failed to load ${configPath}:`, err.message);\\n    }\\n    loadedConfig = [];\\n    return 0;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Transcript persistence ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst transcriptDir = path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"sessions\\\");\\n\\nasync function appendTranscript(name: string, role: \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\", text: string): Promise<void> {\\n  const date = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);\\n  const file = path.join(transcriptDir, `specialist-${name}-${date}.md`);\\n  await mkdir(transcriptDir, { recursive: true });\\n  const prefix = role === \\\"user\\\" ? \\\"**User:**\\\" : `**${name}:**`;\\n  const entry = `${prefix}\\\\n${text}\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n`;\\n  await appendFile(file, entry, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Instance management ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\ninterface LiveInstance {\\n  client: AcpClient;\\n  busy: boolean;\\n  sessionId: string;\\n}\\n\\nconst instances = new Map<string, LiveInstance>();\\n\\nfunction getEntry(name: string): SpecialistEntry | undefined {\\n  return loadedConfig.find((s) => s.name === name);\\n}\\n\\n/** Close and remove a specialist instance. */\\nexport function closeInstance(name: string): void {\\n  const inst = instances.get(name);\\n  if (inst) {\\n    inst.client.close();\\n    instances.delete(name);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] closed (task done)`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Close a parallel instance by its unique instanceId. */\\nexport function closeParallelInstance(instanceId: string): void {\\n  const inst = parallelInstances.get(instanceId);\\n  if (inst) {\\n    inst.client.close();\\n    parallelInstances.delete(instanceId);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] parallel instance closed`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Parallel instances — keyed by unique instanceId, not reused. */\\nconst parallelInstances = new Map<string, LiveInstance>();\\n\\n/** Spawn a fresh instance (no reuse). Returns a unique instanceId. */\\nexport async function spawnNew(name: string, userId?: number): Promise<{ instanceId: string; inst: LiveInstance }> {\\n  const entry = getEntry(name);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown specialist: ${name}`);\\n  return spawnEphemeral(entry, userId);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Spawn a fresh instance from an explicit entry (not looked up in\\n * specialists.json). Used by MoA references (src/moa.ts), which build their\\n * entries from specialist-domains.json at call time. Tracked in\\n * parallelInstances under a unique instanceId — close with closeParallelInstance.\\n */\\nexport async function spawnEphemeral(entry: SpecialistEntry, userId?: number): Promise<{ instanceId: string; inst: LiveInstance }> {\\n  const name = entry.name;\\n  const instanceId = `${name}-${Math.random().toString(16).slice(2, 6)}`;\\n\\n  const opts: AcpClientOptions = {\\n    command: entry.harness.command,\\n    args: [...entry.harness.args],\\n    cwd: path.resolve(entry.cwd),\\n    // TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID rides the same process-env path as MEMORY_DIR — the ACP\\n    // adapter forwards parent env to its MCP stdio children, so the memory server\\n    // resolves the active user id (else list_facts fails \\\"active user id not configured\\\").\\n    env: {\\n      MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n      ...(userId ? { TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID: String(userId) } : {}),\\n      ...entry.env,\\n    },\\n    acpModel: entry.model,\\n    acpEffort: entry.effort,\\n    permissionMode: entry.permissionMode,\\n    promptRetries: 2,\\n    promptRetryBaseMs: 1000,\\n  };\\n\\n  const client = new AcpClient(opts);\\n  client.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n    process.stderr.write(`[specialist:${instanceId}] ${chunk}`);\\n  });\\n  client.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] process exited (code=${code})`);\\n    parallelInstances.delete(instanceId);\\n  });\\n\\n  // If harness uses --agent flag, kiro-cli loads MCP servers from its own\\n  // agent config — passing them again in session/new causes a crash.\\n  const mcpServers = harnessHasAgentFlag(entry) ? undefined : toAcpMcpServers(entry.mcpServers);\\n  const sessionId = await client.initializeAndCreateSession(opts.cwd, mcpServers);\\n  const inst: LiveInstance = { client, busy: false, sessionId };\\n  parallelInstances.set(instanceId, inst);\\n  console.log(`[specialist:${instanceId}] spawned (parallel), session=${sessionId}`);\\n  logEvent(\\\"specialist_spawn\\\", { instanceId, sessionId, parallel: true });\\n  return { instanceId, inst };\\n}\\n\\nasync function spawnOrReuse(name: string, userId?: number): Promise<LiveInstance> {\\n  const existing = instances.get(name);\\n  if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) return existing;\\n\\n  // Clean up stale entry\\n  if (existing) instances.delete(name);\\n\\n  const entry = getEntry(name);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown specialist: ${name}`);\\n\\n  const opts: AcpClientOptions = {\\n    command: entry.harness.command,\\n    args: [...entry.harness.args],\\n    cwd: path.resolve(entry.cwd),\\n    // See spawnNew: TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID rides the same process-env path as MEMORY_DIR\\n    // so the memory MCP child can resolve the active user id.\\n    env: {\\n      MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n      ...(userId ? { TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID: String(userId) } : {}),\\n      ...entry.env,\\n    },\\n    acpModel: entry.model,\\n    acpEffort: entry.effort,\\n    promptRetries: 2,\\n    promptRetryBaseMs: 1000,\\n  };\\n\\n  const client = new AcpClient(opts);\\n  client.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n    process.stderr.write(`[specialist:${name}] ${chunk}`);\\n  });\\n  client.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] process exited (code=${code})`);\\n    instances.delete(name);\\n  });\\n\\n  const sessionId = await client.initializeAndCreateSession(opts.cwd,\\n    harnessHasAgentFlag(entry) ? undefined : toAcpMcpServers(entry.mcpServers));\\n  const inst: LiveInstance = { client, busy: false, sessionId };\\n  instances.set(name, inst);\\n  console.log(`[specialist:${name}] spawned, session=${sessionId}`);\\n  logEvent(\\\"specialist_spawn\\\", { name, sessionId, parallel: false });\\n  return inst;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Dispatch ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface DispatchResult {\\n  payload: ResultPayload;\\n  /** Raw text chunks collected from the specialist's response. */\\n  rawOutput: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Dispatch a delegation to a local specialist.\\n * Spawns/reuses the specialist, sends the goal prompt, collects the response,\\n * and returns a structured ResultPayload.\\n */\\nexport async function dispatchToSpecialist(\\n  target: string,\\n  delegate: DelegatePayload,\\n  userId?: number,\\n): Promise<DispatchResult> {\\n  const entry = getEntry(target);\\n  if (!entry) throw new Error(`[specialist] unknown: ${target}`);\\n\\n  const inst = await spawnOrReuse(target, userId);\\n  if (inst.busy) {\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: \\\"Specialist is busy with another task\\\" },\\n      rawOutput: \\\"\\\",\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  inst.busy = true;\\n  const maxTurns = delegate.maxTurns ?? entry.maxTurns ?? 15;\\n  const timeoutMs = entry.timeoutMs ?? 1_800_000;\\n\\n  // Register with status store\\n  registerTask({\\n    taskId: delegate.id,\\n    type: \\\"specialist\\\",\\n    specialist: target,\\n    goal: delegate.goal.slice(0, 100),\\n  });\\n\\n  // Build preamble from relevant topic shards\\n  const preamble = await buildSpecialistPreamble(target, userId ?? 0).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  // Run scoped enrichment based on the delegation goal\\n  const scope = await getSpecialistScope(target).catch(() => undefined);\\n  const enrichment = await runEnrichment(delegate.goal, userId ?? 0, undefined, scope).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  // Build the goal prompt\\n  const prompt = buildGoalPrompt(delegate, preamble, enrichment);\\n\\n  let output = \\\"\\\";\\n  const updateHandler = (u: SessionUpdate) => {\\n    const up = u.update;\\n    if (\\n      (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" || up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\") \\n    ) {\\n      const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n      if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n        output += content.text;\\n        const role = up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\" ? \\\"thought\\\" : \\\"agent\\\";\\n        broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"chunk\\\", role, text: content.text });\\n      }\\n    }\\n  };\\n  inst.client.on(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n\\n  try {\\n    // Multi-turn loop: keep prompting until agent says done or budget exhausted\\n    let turns = 0;\\n    let lastStopReason = \\\"\\\";\\n\\n    const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\\n\\n    while (turns < maxTurns && Date.now() < deadline) {\\n      turns++;\\n      updateTask(delegate.id, { turns, lastOutput: output.slice(-200) || undefined });\\n      broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status: \\\"running\\\", turns });\\n      const promptText = turns === 1 ? prompt : \\\"[系統] 繼續執行，完成後在回覆中說明結果。\\\";\\n\\n      const result = await Promise.race([\\n        inst.client.prompt(promptText),\\n        new Promise<never>((_, reject) =>\\n          setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(\\\"timeout\\\")), Math.max(1000, deadline - Date.now())),\\n        ),\\n      ]);\\n\\n      lastStopReason = result.stopReason;\\n      // If agent stopped normally (end_turn), check if it needs more turns\\n      if (lastStopReason === \\\"end_turn\\\" || lastStopReason === \\\"stop\\\") break;\\n      // tool_use means agent is still working — loop for next turn\\n    }\\n\\n    // Extract summary from output (last 2000 chars as summary)\\n    const summary = output.length > 2000 ? output.slice(-2000) : output || \\\"(no output)\\\";\\n\\n    const status: ResultPayload[\\\"status\\\"] =\\n      Date.now() >= deadline ? \\\"timeout\\\" : \\\"done\\\";\\n\\n    completeTask(delegate.id, status, { lastOutput: summary.slice(0, 200) });\\n    broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status });\\n\\n    // Persist transcript for /memorytoskill\\n    appendTranscript(target, \\\"user\\\", prompt).catch(() => {});\\n    appendTranscript(target, \\\"assistant\\\", output).catch(() => {});\\n\\n    logEvent(\\\"specialist_done\\\", { target, taskId: delegate.id, status, turns });\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status, summary },\\n      rawOutput: output,\\n    };\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    const msg = err.message ?? String(err);\\n    const failStatus = msg.includes(\\\"timeout\\\") ? \\\"timeout\\\" : \\\"failed\\\";\\n    completeTask(delegate.id, failStatus, { error: msg.slice(0, 200) });\\n    broadcastSSE(delegate.id, { type: \\\"status\\\", status: failStatus });\\n    logEvent(\\\"specialist_done\\\", { target, taskId: delegate.id, status: failStatus, error: msg.slice(0, 100) });\\n    return {\\n      payload: { id: delegate.id, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: `Error: ${msg.slice(0, 500)}` },\\n      rawOutput: output,\\n    };\\n  } finally {\\n    inst.client.removeListener(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n    inst.busy = false;\\n    closeInstance(target);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction buildGoalPrompt(d: DelegatePayload, preamble: string, enrichment?: string): string {\\n  let prompt = preamble;\\n  if (enrichment) prompt += enrichment;\\n  prompt += `[Delegation Task — id: ${d.id}]\\\\n`;\\n  prompt += `Goal: ${d.goal}\\\\n`;\\n  if (d.context) prompt += `Context: ${d.context}\\\\n`;\\n  prompt += `\\\\nComplete this task. When done, summarize what you accomplished.`;\\n  return prompt;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Parallel Dispatch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ParallelTask {\\n  taskId: string;\\n  target: string;\\n  payload: DelegatePayload;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ParallelResult {\\n  taskId: string;\\n  target: string;\\n  status: \\\"done\\\" | \\\"timeout\\\" | \\\"failed\\\";\\n  summary: string;\\n  rawOutput: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Dispatch multiple delegations in parallel. Each gets its own fresh instance.\\n * Returns after all settle (Promise.allSettled).\\n */\\nexport async function dispatchParallel(\\n  tasks: ParallelTask[],\\n  userId?: number,\\n): Promise<ParallelResult[]> {\\n  const promises = tasks.map(async (task): Promise<ParallelResult> => {\\n    const entry = getEntry(task.target);\\n    if (!entry) {\\n      return { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: `Unknown specialist: ${task.target}`, rawOutput: \\\"\\\" };\\n    }\\n\\n    let instanceId = \\\"\\\";\\n    try {\\n      registerTask({\\n        taskId: task.taskId,\\n        type: \\\"parallel_delegate\\\",\\n        specialist: task.target,\\n        goal: task.payload.goal.slice(0, 100),\\n      });\\n      const spawned = await spawnNew(task.target, userId);\\n      instanceId = spawned.instanceId;\\n      const inst = spawned.inst;\\n\\n      const maxTurns = task.payload.maxTurns ?? entry.maxTurns ?? 15;\\n      const timeoutMs = entry.timeoutMs ?? 1_800_000;\\n\\n      const preamble = await buildSpecialistPreamble(task.target, userId ?? 0).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n      const scope = await getSpecialistScope(task.target).catch(() => undefined);\\n      const enrichment = await runEnrichment(task.payload.goal, userId ?? 0, undefined, scope).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n      const prompt = buildGoalPrompt(task.payload, preamble, enrichment);\\n\\n      let output = \\\"\\\";\\n      const updateHandler = (u: SessionUpdate) => {\\n        const up = u.update;\\n        if (\\n          (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" || up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\")\\n        ) {\\n          const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n          if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n            output += content.text;\\n            const role = up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\" ? \\\"thought\\\" : \\\"agent\\\";\\n            broadcastSSE(task.taskId, { type: \\\"chunk\\\", role, text: content.text });\\n          }\\n        }\\n      };\\n      inst.client.on(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n\\n      try {\\n        let turns = 0;\\n        const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\\n\\n        while (turns < maxTurns && Date.now() < deadline) {\\n          turns++;\\n          const promptText = turns === 1 ? prompt : \\\"[系統] 繼續執行，完成後在回覆中說明結果。\\\";\\n          const result = await Promise.race([\\n            inst.client.prompt(promptText),\\n            new Promise<never>((_, reject) =>\\n              setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(\\\"timeout\\\")), Math.max(1000, deadline - Date.now())),\\n            ),\\n          ]);\\n          if (result.stopReason === \\\"end_turn\\\" || result.stopReason === \\\"stop\\\") break;\\n        }\\n\\n        const status: ParallelResult[\\\"status\\\"] = Date.now() >= deadline ? \\\"timeout\\\" : \\\"done\\\";\\n\\n        // Verifier gate (same as RELAY_DELEGATE path)\\n        if (status === \\\"done\\\" && output.length > 50) {\\n          const vr = await verifySpecialistOutput(task.payload.goal, output);\\n          logEvent(\\\"specialist_done\\\", { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, verifier: vr.pass ? \\\"PASS\\\" : \\\"NEEDS_FIX\\\", source: vr.source, issues: vr.issues?.slice(0, 300) });\\n          if (!vr.pass && vr.issues) {\\n            console.log(`[parallel:${task.taskId}] verifier NEEDS_FIX (${vr.source}): ${vr.issues.slice(0, 100)}`);\\n            // Retry once\\n            try {\\n              const retryPrompt = buildRetryPrompt(task.payload.goal, vr.issues);\\n              output = \\\"\\\";\\n              const retryResult = await Promise.race([\\n                inst.client.prompt(retryPrompt),\\n                new Promise<never>((_, reject) => setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(\\\"timeout\\\")), 120_000)),\\n              ]);\\n              void retryResult;\\n            } catch { /* retry failed — use original output */ }\\n          }\\n        }\\n\\n        // summary 必須在 verifier retry 之後才算：retry 會清空 output 重收，\\n        // 提前算會回傳被 verifier 打槍的第一輪輸出，與 rawOutput 不一致。\\n        const summary = output.length > 2000 ? output.slice(-2000) : output || \\\"(no output)\\\";\\n\\n        completeTask(task.taskId, status, { lastOutput: summary.slice(0, 200) });\\n\\n        appendTranscript(task.target, \\\"user\\\", prompt).catch(() => {});\\n        appendTranscript(task.target, \\\"assistant\\\", output).catch(() => {});\\n\\n        return { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, status, summary, rawOutput: output };\\n      } finally {\\n        inst.client.removeListener(\\\"update\\\", updateHandler);\\n        closeParallelInstance(instanceId);\\n      }\\n    } catch (err: any) {\\n      if (instanceId) closeParallelInstance(instanceId);\\n      const msg = (err.message ?? String(err)).slice(0, 500);\\n      completeTask(task.taskId, \\\"failed\\\", { error: msg.slice(0, 200) });\\n      return { taskId: task.taskId, target: task.target, status: \\\"failed\\\", summary: `Error: ${msg}`, rawOutput: \\\"\\\" };\\n    }\\n  });\\n\\n  const settled = await Promise.allSettled(promises);\\n  return settled.map((s, i) =>\\n    s.status === \\\"fulfilled\\\"\\n      ? s.value\\n      : { taskId: tasks[i].taskId, target: tasks[i].target, status: \\\"failed\\\" as const, summary: `Unexpected: ${s.reason}`, rawOutput: \\\"\\\" },\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Cleanup ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport async function shutdownAllSpecialists(): Promise<void> {\\n  for (const [name, inst] of instances) {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:${name}] shutting down...`);\\n    await inst.client.close();\\n  }\\n  instances.clear();\\n  proxyState.clear();\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Transparent Proxy Mode ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst PROXY_DONE_RE = /<<PROXY_DONE>>/i;\\n\\ninterface ProxySession {\\n  specialistName: string;\\n  /** Conversation history for end-of-proxy summary. */\\n  history: { role: \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\"; text: string }[];\\n  startedAt: number;\\n  lastActivity: number;\\n  /** Facts already injected via enrichment — dedup across turns. */\\n  injectedFacts: Set<string>;\\n  /** Total chars sent to specialist (preamble + enrichment + user text). */\\n  charsIn: number;\\n  /** Total chars received from specialist. */\\n  charsOut: number;\\n}\\n\\nconst proxyState = new Map<number, ProxySession>();\\n\\n/** Check if a chatId is currently in proxy mode. */\\nexport function isProxyActive(chatId: number): boolean {\\n  return proxyState.has(chatId);\\n}\\n\\n/** Get the active proxy specialist name for a chatId. */\\nexport function getProxyTarget(chatId: number): string | undefined {\\n  return proxyState.get(chatId)?.specialistName;\\n}\\n\\n/** Get cumulative char stats for the active proxy session. */\\nexport function getProxyStats(chatId: number): { charsIn: number; charsOut: number } | undefined {\\n  const ps = proxyState.get(chatId);\\n  return ps ? { charsIn: ps.charsIn, charsOut: ps.charsOut } : undefined;\\n}\\n\\n/** Enter proxy mode: subsequent user messages go directly to this specialist. */\\nexport function startProxy(chatId: number, specialistName: string): void {\\n  proxyState.set(chatId, {\\n    specialistName,\\n    history: [],\\n    startedAt: Date.now(),\\n    lastActivity: Date.now(),\\n    injectedFacts: new Set(),\\n    charsIn: 0,\\n    charsOut: 0,\\n  });\\n  console.log(`[specialist:proxy] chat ${chatId} → proxy to \\\"${specialistName}\\\"`);\\n}\\n\\n/** Exit proxy mode. Returns the conversation history for summary injection. */\\nexport function endProxy(chatId: number): ProxySession | undefined {\\n  const session = proxyState.get(chatId);\\n  proxyState.delete(chatId);\\n  if (session) {\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] chat ${chatId} exited proxy (${session.history.length} exchanges)`);\\n    closeInstance(session.specialistName);\\n  }\\n  return session;\\n}\\n\\n/** Register a callback for idle timeout auto-exit. */\\n/**\\n * Send a user message to the proxied specialist and collect the response.\\n * Returns the raw output text (caller is responsible for sending to Telegram\\n * and extracting memory tokens).\\n */\\nexport async function promptProxy(\\n  chatId: number,\\n  text: string,\\n  onChunk?: (currentOutput: string) => void,\\n  userId?: number,\\n): Promise<{ output: string; stopReason: string; proxyDone: boolean }> {\\n  const ps = proxyState.get(chatId);\\n  if (!ps) throw new Error(\\\"[specialist:proxy] no active proxy for this chat\\\");\\n\\n  const inst = await spawnOrReuse(ps.specialistName, userId);\\n\\n  // First prompt: prepend preamble + instruction so specialist knows about PROXY_DONE\\n  const isFirst = ps.history.length === 0;\\n  let prompt: string;\\n  if (isFirst) {\\n    const preamble = await buildSpecialistPreamble(ps.specialistName, userId ?? 0, { proxy: true }).catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n    // Run scoped enrichment for the first message too\\n    const scope = await getSpecialistScope(ps.specialistName).catch(() => undefined);\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] enrichment scope for ${ps.specialistName}:`, JSON.stringify(scope ?? null));\\n    console.log(`[specialist:proxy] subsystem ready: embed=${isEmbedModelReady()}, wiki=${isWikiRetrievalReady()}, recall=${isMemoryRecallReady()}`);\\n    const enrichment = await runEnrichment(text, userId ?? \\\" (has content)\\\" : \\\" (empty)\\\"}`);\\n    prompt = `${preamble}[System] You are in a proxy conversation with the user. content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed specialist timeout misclassification in parallel dispatch outer catch</title>
  <subtitle>Outer catch block now classifies timeout errors correctly instead of always reporting failed status</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>src/specialist.ts dispatchParallel outer catch block previously hardcoded status as "failed"</fact>
    <fact>Promise.race timeout rejections throw errors with "timeout" in the message</fact>
    <fact>Added timeout detection logic matching runSpecialistTask pattern: msg.includes("timeout") checks determine status</fact>
    <fact>Timeout errors now correctly return status "timeout" instead of "failed" in parallel dispatch error path</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed timeout misclassification in the parallel dispatch error handler. The outer catch block in dispatchParallel was hardcoded to always return status "failed", even when the error came from Promise.race timeout rejection. This caused timeout errors to be misreported as generic failures. The fix adds the same timeout detection pattern used in runSpecialistTask: checking if the error message includes "timeout" and setting failStatus accordingly. This ensures consistent timeout reporting across both serial and parallel specialist dispatch paths.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/specialist.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/specialist.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

Genuine later background output re-arms fresh.\\n    if (s._orphanFlushTimer) { clearTimeout(s._orphanFlushTimer); s._orphanFlushTimer = undefined; }\\n    s._orphanBuffer = undefined;\\n    // Suppress capture across the force-kill window so trailing chunks don't leak.\\n    s._orphanSuppressUntil = Date.now() + 20_000;\\n\\n    // Force-kill escalation: if CLI doesn't exit the turn within 15s,\\n    // kill the process tree and re-create on next message.\\n    if (!s.client.isClosed) {\\n      const timer = setTimeout(async () => {\\n        if (s.client.isClosed) return;\\n        console.warn(`[cancel] chat ${chatId} CLI still alive after 15s — force-killing`);\\n        await s.client.close();\\n      }, 15_000);\\n      timer.unref?.();\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Enable drop-all mode: silently discard messages sent before now. */\\n  enableDropAll(chatId: number) { this._dropBefore.set(chatId, Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)); }\\n  /** Disable drop-all mode. */\\n  disableDropAll(chatId: number) { this._dropBefore.delete(chatId); }\\n  /** Check if a message should be dropped (sent before the cutoff). */\\n  shouldDrop(chatId: number, messageDate: number): boolean {\\n    const cutoff = this._dropBefore.get(chatId);\\n    if (!cutoff) return false;\\n    if (messageDate <= cutoff) return true;\\n    // Message is newer than cutoff — auto-clear and let it through\\n    this._dropBefore.delete(chatId);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n\\n  peek(chatId: number): ChatSession | undefined {\\n    return this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n  }\\n\\n  size(): number {\\n    return this.sessions.size;\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Persist a closing session's archive, or carry the prior archive forward.\\n   *\\n   * A \\\"substantive\\\" close (>= config.archiveMinTurns turns with some assistant\\n   * output) is exported — including a close-time LLM summary — and saved. itself skips the LLM summary for crash /\\n   * health_monitor closes (process dying) and falls back to truncation.\\n   */\\n  private async archiveOnClose(s: ChatSession, closeReason: SessionArchive[\\\"closeReason\\\"]) {\\n    const assistantChars = s.history.reduce(\\n      (n, h) => (h.role === \\\"assistant\\\" ? n + h.text.length : n),\\n      0,\\n    );\\n    const substantive = s.history.length >= config.archiveMinTurns && assistantChars > 0;\\n    try {\\n      if (substantive) {\\n        await saveArchive(await exportSession(s, closeReason));\\n      } else if (s.priorArchive) {\\n        // Trivial close — keep the prior, more-substantive archive.\\n        await saveArchive(s.priorArchive);\\n        console.log(`[archive] chat ${s.chatId}: trivial close (${s.history.length} turns), carried prior archive forward`);\\n      } else if (s.history.length > 0) {\\n        // No prior to fall back to — persist what little we have.\\n        await saveArchive(await exportSession(s, closeReason));\\n      }\\n    } catch (e) {\\n      console.error(`[session ${s.chatId}] archive failed:`, e);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  async drop(chatId: number, closeReason: \\\"manual\\\" | \\\"reset\\\" | \\\"restart\\\" | \\\"agent_switch\\\" = \\\"manual\\\") {\\n    const s = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (!s) return;\\n    this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n    // manual/reset/restart/agent_switch 都是「要新 context」的明確意圖 →\\n    // 清 resume 記錄。idle/crash/health_monitor 不走 drop()，記錄保留。\\n    if (config.acpSessionResume) {\\n      await clearResumable(chatId).catch(() => {});\\n    }\\n    // Archive session state before closing (summary + trivial carry-forward)\\n    if (s.history.length > 0 || s.priorArchive) {\\n      await this.archiveOnClose(s, closeReason);\\n    }\\n    if (this.onBeforeClose) {\\n      try { await this.onBeforeClose(s); } catch (e) {\\n        console.error(`[session ${chatId}] onBeforeClose failed:`, e);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    await s.client.close();\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Park — 暫停 session（/session 切換用）：關 subprocess、record 保留，\\n   * 之後可 session/load 切回。與 drop() 對照：drop = fresh 意圖（清 active\\n   * record + archive + onBeforeClose 全套收尾），park 刻意都不做——\\n   * onBeforeClose 含 writeWorkingState(\\\"session closed\\\") + saveArchive，\\n   * archive 會被下次 create 消費刪除且 resume 路徑直接丟棄（純汙染）。\\n   * 但單獨存 transcript（fire-and-forget），讓 parked 段落仍進 memory\\n   * extraction 素材。無活 session 時 no-op（idle sweep 已收掉的場景）。\\n   */\\n  async park(chatId: number): Promise<void> {\\n reason: \\\"park\\\" }, { chatId, userId: s.userId });\\n    if (s.history.length > 0) {\\n      // dynamic import 防 sessionManager ⇄ session-extract 循環依賴\\n      import(\\\"./session-extract.js\\\")\\n        .then((m) => m.saveTranscript(s))\\n        .catch((e) => console.warn(`[session ${chatId}] park transcript failed:`, e));\\n    }\\n    await s.client.close();\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * /session 切換：park 現行 → setActive → create()（resolveResumeCandidate\\n   * 自然命中新 active，走 session/load 恢復目標 context）。\\n   * 順序防呆：① 目標先驗證存在（read-only，不存在時現行 records.find((r) => r.id === targetId);\\n    if (!target) {\\n      throw new Error(`找不到 session ${targetId}（可能已被移除），請重開 /session 列表`);\\n    }\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n newSession(chatId: number, userId?: number, name?: string): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing?.inflight) {\\n      throw new Error(\\\"目前有 turn 進行中，請等它完成或先 /cancel 再開新對話\\\");\\n    }\\n    const { sessions: records } = await listSessions(chatId);\\n    if (records.length >= MAX_SESSIONS_PER_CHAT) {\\n      throw new Error(`已達 session content.thinking: This is fixing the switchProvider function to add an inflight check before closing the old client. The fix adds the same guard as switchAgent/switchSession/newSession - checking if a turn is in progress and throwing an error if so, to prevent the "ACP agent exited" error from closing the client mid-turn.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Added inflight guard to switchProvider to prevent mid-turn closure</title>
  <subtitle>switchProvider now checks for in-flight turns before closing client, matching switchAgent/switchSession/newSession behavior</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>src/sessionManager.ts switchProvider previously lacked inflight check before closing the old client</fact>
    <fact>Closing client during active runPrompt causes "ACP agent exited" error</fact>
    <fact>switchAgent, switchSession, and newSession already had this inflight guard pattern</fact>
    <fact>switchProvider now throws error when turn is in progress, instructing user to wait or /cancel first</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed a race condition in switchProvider where switching providers during an active turn would close the old client immediately, causing the in-progress runPrompt to fail with "ACP agent exited" error. The fix adds the same inflight guard used by switchAgent, switchSession, and newSession: checking existing?.inflight and throwing a user-friendly error message instructing them to wait for completion or /cancel before switching. This prevents mid-turn disruption and aligns all session-switching methods with the same safety pattern.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

The user committed three bug fixes to the repository:

1. **Specialist parallel timeout classification**: Fixed the outer catch block in dispatchParallel to classify timeout errors correctly (was always marking as "failed")

2. **Specialist verifier retry summary recalculation**: Moved summary calculation to after verifier retry block so it captures retry output instead of rejected first attempt

3. **SessionManager switchProvider inflight guard**: Added inflight check to switchProvider to prevent mid-turn client closure

This is a significant completed work - three bugfixes implemented, verified with TypeScript compilation, and committed to git.

content.thinking:
The user is looking at the askRegistry usage across the codebase. This is task #5 which is about fixing askRegistry 無界成長 (unbounded growth). They're investigating where askRegistry is used to understand how to add TTL sweep or LRU limit.

From the grep results, I can see:
- askRegistry is defined in telegram-ui.ts as `new Map<number, AskEntry[]>()`
- It's used in:
  - telegram-ui.ts: set when creating ask keyboards
  - index.ts: get to retrieve entries, delete after use
  - run-prompt.ts: set when creating ask keyboards
  - commands/relay.ts: set when creating ask keyboards

The pattern is:
- Set when sending messages with ASK keyboards (keyed by message_id)
- Get when handling callback queries (to find the question/option)
- Delete after callback is answered

The issue is: if a message with ASK keyboard is never answered (user ignores it, bot crashes, etc.), the entry stays in the map forever.

The fix would be to add either:
1. TTL-based cleanup (sweep entries older than X hours)
2. LRU limit (keep only last N entries)

This is investigation work, not actual implementation yet.

content.thinking:
The user is investigating the askRegistry unbounded growth issue. They're reading the code around where askRegistry is defined and used. This is task #5 from the handoff document.

The issue is that askRegistry is a Map that grows unbounded - entries are added when ASK keyboards are sent, but they're only deleted when the user clicks a button (index.ts:359). If users don't click buttons, or if the bridge restarts, the registry grows forever.

The fix should add either:
1. TTL-based cleanup (entries older than X time get swept)
2. LRU with a size cap
3. Both

Let me wait to see what fix is implemented.

`${mDirect[1]}:${mDirect[2]}`;\\n  if (kind === \\\"at\\\") {\\n    const mTmr = /^tmr_(\\\\d{2})(\\\\d{2})$/.exec(key);\\n    if (mTmr) return `tomorrow ${mTmr[1]}:${mTmr[2]}`;\\n  }\\n  return null;\\n}\\n\\nexport function describePendingSchedule(kind: PendingSchedule[\\\"kind\\\"], time: string): string {\\n  switch (kind) {\\n    case \\\"add\\\":   return `${time} 後（一次性）`;\\n    case \\\"at\\\":    return `${time}（一次性）`;\\n    case \\\"every\\\": return `每 ${time}`;\\n    case \\\"daily\\\": return `每天 ${time}`;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\nexport async function sendFilesByPaths(ctx: Context, paths: string[]): Promise<number> {\\n  if (paths.length === 0) return 0;\\n  const chatId = ctx.chat?.id;\\n  const seen = new Set<string>();\\n  let sent = 0;\\n  for (const raw of paths) {\\n    const abs = path.resolve(raw);\\n    const key = abs.toLowerCase();\\n    if (seen.has(key)) continue;\\n    seen.add(key);\\n    if (!isUnderAllowedRoot(abs)) {\\n      console.log(`[chat ${chatId}] SEND_FILE: refused (not under allowed roots): ${abs}`);\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    try {\\n      const st = await stat(abs);\\n      if (!st.isFile()) {\\n        console.log(`[chat ${chatId}] SEND_FILE: skip (not a regular file): ${abs}`);\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      const ext = path.extname(abs).toLowerCase();\\n      // .md → .txt display name: Telegram in-app viewer 對 .md UTF-8 偵測不可靠，中文會亂碼\\n      const displayName = ext === \\\".md\\\"\\n        ? path.basename(abs, ext) + \\\".txt\\\"\\n        : undefined;\\n      const asPhoto = IMG_EXT.has(ext) && ext !== \\\".png\\\" && st.size <= 10 * 1024 * ${abs}`);\\n      }\\n      sent++;\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.warn(`[chat ${chatId}] SEND_FILE: failed ${abs} — ${(err as Error).message}`);\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return sent;\\n}\\n\\nexport {\\n    const r = path.resolve(root).toLowerCase().replace(/[\\\\\\\\/]+$/, \\\"\\\");\\n    if (normAbs === r) return true;\\n    return normAbs.startsWith(r + \\\"\\\\\\\\\\\") || lines = piece.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\");\\n    let inside = false;\\n    let lang = \\\"\\\";\\n    for (const line of lines) {\\n      const m = line.match(fenceRe);\\n      if (!m) continue;\\n      if (!inside) {\\n        inside = true;\\n        lang = m[1] ?? \\\"\\\";\\n      } else {\\n        inside = false;\\n        lang = \\\"\\\";\\n      }\\n    }\\n    if (inside && i < raw.length - 1) {\\n      piece = piece + (piece.endsWith(\\\"\\\\n\\\") ? \\\"\\\" : \\\"\\\\n\\\") + \\\"```\\\";\\n      openLang = lang;\\n    } else {\\n      openLang = \\\"\\\";\\n    }\\n    out.push(piece);\\n  }\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// renderReply (streaming display)\\n// renderReply(\\n  session: ChatSession,\\n  done: boolean,\\n  parsers: ScheduleParsers,\\n): RenderResult {\\n  const toolLines = toolPrefixLines(session);\\n  const tools = toolLines.length ? \\\"\\\";\\n  if (session.thinkingBuffer) {\\n    const t = session.thinkingBuffer;\\n    const preview = t.length > 200 ? \\\"…\\\" + t.slice(-200) : t;\\n    thinkingBlock = \\\"💭 \\\" + content.thinking:
The user made a significant change to the askRegistry implementation. Let me analyze what happened:

1. **Before**: askRegistry was a simple `Map<number, AskEntry[]>()` that only deleted entries when buttons were clicked
2. **After**: Created a bounded AskRegistry class with:
   - TTL (24 hours) for lazy expiration on get()
   - Max entries limit (500) with LRU eviction on set()
   - No sweep timer - expiration happens lazily
   - Prevents unbounded memory growth from unclicked keyboards

This is a fix for the "askRegistry 無界成長" issue (medium #4 from the task list).

The implementation is clever:
- Uses Map insertion order (≈ time order) for simple LRU
- Lazy expiration on get() checks TTL
- Set evicts oldest entries when at capacity
- Expired button clicks fall back to existing key-only path (same as after bridge restart)

This is a memory leak fix - keyboards that are never clicked would accumulate forever in the old implementation.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed askRegistry unbounded memory growth with TTL and LRU bounds</title>
  <subtitle>Replaced raw Map with bounded AskRegistry class using 24h TTL and 500-entry LRU eviction</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>src/telegram-ui.ts askRegistry previously used raw Map with no cleanup except on button click</fact>
    <fact>Unclicked keyboards would accumulate indefinitely, causing memory leak in long-running bridge processes</fact>
    <fact>New AskRegistry class implements dual bounds: 24-hour TTL and 500-entry maximum</fact>
    <fact>Lazy expiration on get() checks TTL; set() evicts oldest entries when at capacity using Map insertion order</fact>
    <fact>No sweep timer needed; expired button clicks fall back to key-only path matching bridge restart behavior</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed memory leak in askRegistry where ASK keyboard metadata accumulated unbounded. The original implementation used a raw Map that only deleted entries when buttons were clicked - unclicked keyboards (the common case) would persist forever, leaking memory in long-running bridge processes. Replaced with bounded AskRegistry class implementing dual safeguards: a 24-hour TTL with lazy expiration on get(), and a 500-entry LRU cap that evicts oldest entries on set(). No background sweep timer needed. Expired button clicks gracefully degrade to the existing key-only fallback path (same behavior as after bridge restart), so old keyboards remain functional even after expiry.</narrative>

  <narrative>Committed fix for askRegistry memory leak. The original raw Map implementation had a single deletion point (button clicks), causing unclicked keyboards to accumulate forever in long-running bridge processes. The new bounded AskRegistry class enforces dual limits: a 24-hour TTL with lazy expiration at get() time, and a 500-entry maximum with LRU eviction at set() time. No background sweep timer needed. Expired button clicks degrade gracefully to the existing key-only fallback path (same as post-restart behavior). The interface (set/get/delete) remains identical, requiring no changes to the 8 existing call sites, and TypeScript compilation confirms no other Map methods were in use.</narrative>

Must exactly match a value the selected model supports —\\n   * unlike model, there is no fuzzy fallback. Empty/undefined = untouched.\\n   */\\n  acpEffort?: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Errors where the agent's cloud backend aborted the response stream. These\\n * are almost always worth retrying (network blip, TLS reset, 5xx from the\\n * upstream provider). We detect by error code + message/data fingerprints,\\n * since kiro-cli wraps them as generic JSON-RPC -32603 Internal error.\\n */\\nfunction isTransientBackendError(err: unknown): boolean {\\n  if (!err || typeof err !== \\\"object\\\") return false;\\n  const e = err as { code?: number; message?: string; data?: unknown };\\n  if (e.code !== -32603) return false;\\n  const parts: string[] = [];\\n  if (typeof e.message === \\\"string\\\") parts.push(e.message);\\n  if (e.data !== undefined) {\\n    try {\\n      parts.push(typeof e.data === \\\"string\\\" ? e.data : JSON.stringify(e.data));\\n    } catch {\\n      /* ignore */\\n    }\\n  }\\n  const blob = parts.join(\\\" \\\").toLowerCase();\\n  return (\\n    blob.includes(\\\"dispatch failure\\\") ||\\n    blob.includes(\\\"response stream\\\") ||\\n    blob.includes(\\\"timeout\\\") ||\\n    blob.includes(\\\"connection reset\\\") ||\\n    blob.includes(\\\"connection closed\\\") ||\\n    blob.includes(\\\"broken pipe\\\")\\n  );\\n}\\n\\ntype Framing = \\\"line\\\" | \\\"lsp\\\";\\n\\nexport declare interface AcpClient {\\n  on(event: \\\"update\\\", listener: (u: SessionUpdate) => void): this;\\n  on(event: \\\"exit\\\", listener: (code: number | null) => void): this;\\n  on(event: \\\"stderr\\\", listener: (chunk: string) => void): this;\\n  on(event: \\\"request\\\", listener: (req: JsonRpcRequest) => void): this;\\n}\\n\\nexport class AcpClient extends EventEmitter {\\n  private proc: ChildProcessWithoutNullStreams;\\n  private nextId = 1;\\n  private pending = new Map<\\n    JsonRpcId,\\n    { resolve: (v: unknown) => void; reject: (e: Error) => void }\\n  >();\\n  /** Incoming bytes, kept as Buffer because LSP framing needs byte counts. */\\n  private inBuf: Buffer = Buffer.alloc(0);\\n  /** Outbound framing. Everything else (node, npx.exe, a real .exe)\\n    // can be spawned without a shell — which avoids Node's DEP0190 warning\\n    // about passing args alongside `shell: true` and also sidesteps any\\n    // shell-quoting surprises in the args array.\\n    const needsShell = AcpClient.commandNeedsShell(opts.command);\\n    if (needsShell) {\\n      // When shell is required, build a single quoted command string and\\n      // pass empty args — that's the API shape Node wants post-DEP0190.\\n      const cmdLine = [opts.command, ...opts.args]\\n        .map(AcpClient.quoteForShell)\\n        .join(\\\" \\\");\\n      this.proc = spawn(cmdLine, [], {\\n        cwd: opts.cwd,\\n        env: buildSpawnEnv(opts.env),\\n        stdio: [\\\"pipe\\\", \\\"pipe\\\", \\\"pipe\\\"],\\n        shell: true,\\n      });\\n    } else {\\n      this.proc = spawn(opts.command, opts.args, {\\n        cwd: opts.cwd,\\n        env: buildSpawnEnv(opts.env),\\n        stdio: [\\\"pipe\\\", \\\"pipe\\\", \\\"pipe\\\"],\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    // Keep stdout as Buffer so LSP byte-length framing works correctly.\\n    this.proc.stderr.setEncoding(\\\"utf8\\\");\\n\\n    this.proc.stdout.on(\\\"data\\\", (chunk: Buffer) => this.onStdout(chunk));\\n    this.proc.stderr.on(\\\"data\\\", (chunk: string) => this.emit(\\\"stderr\\\", chunk));\\n    this.proc.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n      this.closed = true;\\n      for (const { reject } of this.pending.values()) {\\n        reject(new Error(`ACP agent exited with code ${code}`));\\n      }\\n      this.pending.clear();\\n      this.emit(\\\"exit\\\", code);\\n    });\\n    this.proc.on(\\\"error\\\", (err) => {\\n      this.emit(\\\"stderr\\\", `spawn error: ${err.message}\\\\n`);\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  private onStdout(chunk: Buffer) {\\n    this.inBuf = this.inBuf.length === 0 ? msg.error.data === \\\"string\\\"\\n                ? msg.error.data\\n                : JSON.stringify(msg.error.data);\\n            if (s && s !== \\\"{}\\\" && s !== \\\"null\\\") detail = ` (${s})`;\\n          } catch {\\n            /* ignore stringify errors */\\n          }\\n        }\\n        const err = new Error(\\n          `${msg.error.code}: ${msg.error.message}${detail}`\\n String(err);\\n        this.emit(\\n          \\\"stderr\\\",\\n          `[acp] set ${configId}=${value} not applied (ignored): ${msg}\\\\n`,\\n        );\\n      }\\n    };\\n\\n    if (model) await set(\\\"model\\\", model);\\n    if (effort) await set(\\\"effort\\\", effort);\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Send a user prompt and wait for the turn to end.\\n   * Text chunks streamed by the agent are delivered via \\\"update\\\" events.\\n   *\\n   * Automatically retries transient backend errors (see\\n   * isTransientBackendError) up to `opts.promptRetries` times with\\n   * exponential backoff. content.thinking:
This is a significant refactoring of the acpClient.ts close() method. Let me understand what changed:

1. Import change: Added `execFile` to the imports
2. Major changes to close() method:
   - Immediately sets `this.closed = true` and rejects all pending promises before attempting to kill the process
   - This prevents promises from hanging if the kill doesn't work (e.g., PID recycled)
   - On Windows, replaced synchronous `execSync` with async `execFile` for taskkill
   - This avoids blocking the event loop during multi-session shutdown
   - The async version wraps execFile in a Promise that always resolves
   - If taskkill fails, it falls back to direct proc.kill()

This is fixing two issues from the medium priority list:
1. close() blocking issue - execSync blocks event loop
2. pending promises hanging - if kill fails, promises were never rejected

This is a solid fix that addresses the race conditions and blocking behavior.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed acpClient.close() blocking and pending promise hangs</title>
  <subtitle>Immediately rejects pending promises and uses async execFile instead of blocking execSync for Windows taskkill</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>src/acpClient.ts close() now immediately sets closed=true and rejects all pending promises before kill</fact>
    <fact>Previous implementation left promises hanging if kill failed or PID was recycled</fact>
    <fact>Windows taskkill changed from blocking execSync to async execFile to prevent event loop stalls during multi-session shutdown</fact>
    <fact>Exit handler cleanup is now idempotent - pending map already cleared by close()</fact>
    <fact>execFile callback includes fallback to proc.kill() if taskkill fails or PID doesn't exist</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed two critical issues in acpClient.close(). First, pending session/prompt promises could hang forever if process kill failed (e.g., PID recycled and reassigned to unrelated process). Now close() immediately sets closed flag and rejects all pending promises with "ACP agent closed" error before attempting kill, ensuring callers never deadlock regardless of kill outcome. Exit handler cleanup becomes idempotent since pending map is already cleared. Second, Windows taskkill used blocking execSync which could stall event loop during multi-session shutdown (e.g., bridge restart). Replaced with async execFile wrapped in Promise, allowing concurrent session cleanup without serialization. Callback includes fallback to direct proc.kill() if taskkill fails, maintaining same reliability as before but without blocking.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/acpClient.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/acpClient.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Committed acpClient.close() async taskkill and immediate pending rejection fix</title>
  <subtitle>Prevents event loop blocking and promise hangs during session shutdown</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Commit 8a8d6df refactors acpClient.close() to use async execFile instead of blocking execSync</fact>
    <fact>close() now immediately sets closed=true and rejects all pending promises before attempting kill</fact>
    <fact>Multi-session shutdown no longer serializes on blocking taskkill calls, preventing event loop stalls</fact>
    <fact>Pending promises no longer hang when kill fails or PID is recycled to unrelated process</fact>
    <fact>Exit handler cleanup becomes idempotent since pending map already cleared by close()</fact>
    <fact>Existing closed checks in sendRequest/retry/cancel remain semantically correct</fact>
    <fact>Grep verification confirmed no callers compare rejection message strings</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Committed fix for two critical acpClient.close() issues. The blocking execSync taskkill has been replaced with async execFile wrapped in a Promise, allowing concurrent session cleanup during multi-session shutdown (e.g., bridge restart) without serializing on the event loop. More critically, close() now immediately sets the closed flag and rejects all pending promises with "ACP agent closed" before attempting process kill. This prevents permanent promise hangs when kill fails or the PID gets recycled to an unrelated process - the caller's inflight promises resolve with rejection instead of deadlocking. The exit handler's pending cleanup becomes idempotent since the map is already cleared by close(). All existing closed-state checks remain correct, and grep verification confirmed no code paths compare rejection message strings. TypeScript compilation passed cleanly.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/acpClient.ts</file>
    <file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
    <file>src/session-extract.ts</file>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/acpClient.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

Looking at the grep results, I can see that:
1. `cfg.targetBotUsername` is the configured username for the telegram transport (e.g., "@RK_AI_Codex_bot")
2. `cfg.botName` is this bot's own name
3. The code currently checks `if (targetBot === cfg.botName)` to prevent self-relay
4. But there's no check that `targetBot` matches `cfg.targetBotUsername` for telegram transport

The issue is that in the telegram transport path (lines 173-182 from the grep), it uses `cfg.targetBotUsername` directly without checking if the requested `targetBot` parameter matches it. This means if an agent says "relay to @SomeOtherBot" but the config has `targetBotUsername: "@ConfiguredBot"`, it will still send to `@ConfiguredBot`, silently ignoring the agent's intention.

Let me wait for the actual code changes to see how this is being fixed.

content.thinking:
User is investigating relay targetBot validation. The grep shows that relay.ts uses `cfg.targetBotUsername` for telegram transport, and config.ts loads it from `RELAY_TARGET_BOT_USERNAME`. The issue from the handoff document is that the telegram path ignores targetBot and always sends to cfg.targetBotUsername, even when targetBot is different.

Looking at the code:
- sendRelay() receives `targetBot` as parameter (the destination bot name from RELAY_DELEGATE token)
- But the telegram path at line 173-180 uses `cfg.targetBotUsername` instead of validating that targetBot matches it
- This means if agent says <<RELAY_DELEGATE: wrong-bot | ...>>, the message still goes to targetBotUsername without warning

The fix should be: in the telegram path, check if targetBot matches cfg.botName's expected peer, and if not, warn/refuse instead of silently sending to the wrong destination.

Since there's no direct "targetBot config → allowed peers" map, the medium finding's suggestion is to validate targetBot against cfg metadata when using telegram transport.

content.thinking:
The user is implementing a fix for relay telegram path targetBot validation. They've added a peerUsernames map in the config that parses RELAY_PEER_USERNAMES environment variable to map peer names to their telegram usernames. This allows multiple peer mappings instead of a single targetBotUsername.

The comment explains:
- Format: "小白=@bot_a,小X=@bot_b"
- If not set → telegram path uses single targetBotUsername (existing behavior)
- If set → resolve username by peer name, reject peers not in table to avoid sending to wrong bot

This is part of fixing medium #5 from the handoff: "relay telegram 路徑 targetBot 與 cfg 不符時警告拒送"

content.text: No output necessary - code modification adding peerUsernames configuration for multi-peer relay mapping.

content.thinking:
The user is modifying the relay configuration to add a peerUsernames field that maps peer names to Telegram usernames for multi-peer relay support. This is part of fixing the issue where telegram relay ignored targetBot and always sent to the configured targetBotUsername.

The change adds an optional peerUsernames mapping in the RelayConfig interface, which will be used to resolve the correct Telegram username for each peer.

content.text: No output necessary - interface update adding peerUsernames field to RelayConfig.

content.thinking:
The user is adding a function `resolvePeerUsername` to resolve the Telegram username for a specific peer. This function handles both single-peer (original behavior using targetBotUsername) and multi-peer (using peerUsernames map) configurations. If the map is set but doesn't contain the peer, it returns null to prevent accidentally sending to the default bot.

This is part of fixing the relay targetBot validation issue mentioned in the medium findings list.

content.thinking:
The user is modifying the relay sendRelay function to use the new resolvePeerUsername helper instead of directly using cfg.targetBotUsername. This changes the Telegram path to:
1. Resolve the peer's username from the peerUsernames map (or fall back to targetBotUsername if map not configured)
2. If the peer is in the map, send to the resolved username
3. If the peer is NOT in the map (when map is configured), log a warning and skip telegram transport
4. When transport=both, this naturally falls back to redis for unlisted peers

This is the actual fix for medium #5 - ensuring telegram relay messages go to the correct peer instead of always using the single targetBotUsername.

content.text: No output necessary - sendRelay function updated to use peer-specific username resolution with validation warning.

The user is making edits to the relay.ts file to add peer-specific username resolution. They've:

3. Updated sendRelay to use this helper instead of hardcoded targetBotUsername

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed relay telegram ignoring targetBot with multi-peer username mapping</title>
  <subtitle>Telegram transport now resolves peer-specific usernames via RELAY_PEER_USERNAMES, preventing messages from being misdirected to default bot</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>src/config.ts added RELAY_PEER_USERNAMES environment variable parsing for peer-to-username mapping</fact>
    <fact>src/relay.ts RelayConfig interface gained optional peerUsernames field for multi-peer configuration</fact>
    <fact>resolvePeerUsername() helper resolves telegram username per peer: uses map when configured, falls back to targetBotUsername for single-peer mode</fact>
    <fact>Peers not in configured map return null, preventing silent misdirection to default bot when using transport=both (gracefully falls back to redis)</fact>
    <fact>sendRelay and sendRelayFile now use resolvePeerUsername(targetBot) instead of hardcoded cfg.targetBotUsername</fact>
    <fact>Warning logs identify peer name and configuration mismatch for debugging</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed relay telegram transport ignoring the targetBot parameter and always sending to a single hardcoded username. Added RELAY_PEER_USERNAMES environment variable supporting comma-separated "peer=@username" pairs for multi-peer mapping (e.g., "小白=@bot_a,小X=@bot_b"). New resolvePeerUsername() helper checks the map first; if configured and peer exists, returns mapped username; if map empty/unset, falls back to single targetBotUsername (backward compatible); if map configured but peer absent, returns null to prevent silent misdirection. When null, transport=telegram fails with warning; transport=both gracefully falls back to redis for correct delivery. Both sendRelay and sendRelayFile updated to use the resolver, with enhanced logging showing both resolved username and original peer name for traceability.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/config.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/config.ts</file>
    <file>src/relay.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

= path.resolve(import.meta.dirname ?? \\\".\\\", \\\"..\\\", \\\"status-app\\\");\\n\\n  // 前端一律以 location.origin 同源 fetch（localhost 直開 / Mini App tunnel 皆同源），\\n  // 不設 CORS header——避免任意網頁跨源讀取本機 content.thinking:
The user committed the relay targetBot fix. This is a bugfix that addresses the relay telegram transport ignoring the targetBot parameter and always sending to a hardcoded username. The commit message explains the issue and the solution clearly.

Let me create an observation for this commit.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Committed relay telegram peer username resolution fix for multi-peer misdirection</title>
  <subtitle>Telegram transport now resolves peer-specific usernames preventing silent message misdirection in multi-peer deployments</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Commit 3c95607 fixes relay telegram transport ignoring targetBot parameter and always using hardcoded cfg.targetBotUsername</fact>
    <fact>Added RELAY_PEER_USERNAMES environment variable supporting comma-separated peer-to-username mapping</fact>
    <fact>resolvePeerUsername() helper provides backward-compatible single-peer fallback when map unconfigured</fact>
    <fact>Peers not in configured map are rejected from telegram transport with warning, gracefully falling back to redis for transport=both</fact>
    <fact>Commit message notes that comparing targetBot vs targetBotUsername (original review suggestion) is infeasible as they're different namespaces</fact>
    <fact>TypeScript compilation verified clean before commit</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Committed fix for relay telegram transport silently misdirecting messages in multi-peer scenarios. The original implementation ignored the targetBot parameter entirely, always sending to cfg.targetBotUsername regardless of intended peer. In multi-peer setups, messages meant for peer B would incorrectly go to the single configured bot while group mirrors misleadingly showed "-> peer B", making debugging difficult. The fix adds optional RELAY_PEER_USERNAMES environment variable for comma-separated peer-to-username mappings. When unconfigured, behavior falls back to single targetBotUsername (backward compatible). When configured, peers are resolved via the map; unmapped peers are rejected from telegram transport with warning and gracefully fall back to redis (which already routes correctly by peer name). The commit message explicitly addresses why the original review suggestion (comparing targetBot vs targetBotUsername) won't work: they're different namespaces that never literally match.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/relay.ts</file>
    <file>src/config.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/relay.ts</file>
    <file>src/config.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

  <outcome>"{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\status-server.ts\",\"oldString\":\"import { createServer, type Server, type IncomingMessage, type ServerResponse } from \\\"node:http\\\";\\nimport { readFile, readdir, stat } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\",\"newString\":\"import { createServer, type Server, type IncomingMessage, type ServerResponse } from \\\"node:http\\\";\\nimport { readFile, readdir, stat } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport { timingSafeEqual } from \\\"node:crypto\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\",\"originalFile\":\"/**\\n * Status Server — lightweight HTTP API + Mini App for /status monitoring.\\n * Independent server on STATUS_PORT (default 3847).\\n */\\nimport { createServer, type Server, type IncomingMessage, type ServerResponse } from \\\"node:http\\\";\\nimport { readFile, readdir, stat } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { listTasks, listActiveTasks, getTask, cancelTask } from \\\"./status-store.js\\\";\\nimport { getSpecialists, type SpecialistEntry } from \\\"./specialist.js\\\";\\nimport { getRecentEvents, type StateEvent } from \\\"./event-log.js\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\n\\nlet httpServer: Server | undefined;\\n\\n// SSE clients per taskId\\nconst sseClients = new Map<string, Set<ServerResponse>>();\\n\\n// ─── Specialist helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** decodeURIComponent 對無效 percent-encoding（%zz）會 throw URIError，統一轉成 null 讓路由回 400。 */\\nfunction safeDecode(s: string): string | null {\\n  try { return decodeURIComponent(s); } catch { return null; }\\n}\\n\\nfunction escapeRegex(s: string): string {\\n  return s.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\\\\]\\\\\\\\]/g, \\\"\\\\\\\\$&\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/** env 可能含 API key 等機密，回傳前把機密欄位遮罩。 */\\nfunction maskEnv(env?: Record<string, string>): Record<string, string> | undefined {\\n  if (!env) return env;\\n  return Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(env).map(([k, v]) =>\\n    [k, /key|token|secret|password|credential/i.test(k) ? \\\"••••••\\\" : v]));\\n}\\n\\nfunction extractModel(entry: SpecialistEntry): string {\\n  const args = entry.harness.args;\\n  const idx = args.indexOf(\\\"--model\\\");\\n  return idx >= 0 && idx + 1 < args.length ? args[idx + 1] : \\\"default\\\";\\n}\\n\\nfunction countRecentTasks(name: string, events: StateEvent[], days: number): number {\\n  const cutoff = new Date();\\n  cutoff.setDate(cutoff.getDate() - days);\\n  const cutoffStr = cutoff.toISOString();\\n  return events.filter(\\n    (e) => e.type === \\\"specialist_spawn\\\" && e.ts >= cutoffStr &&\\n      ((e.payload.name as string) === name || (e.payload.target as string) === name)\\n  ).length;\\n}\\n\\nlet factsCache: { ts: number; recentFacts: Array<{ id: string; timestamp: string; text: string }> }> {\\n  const now = Date.now();\\n  if (!factsCache || now - factsCache.ts > FACTS_CACHE_TTL) {\\n    factsCache = { ts: now, data: new Map() };\\n    const userId = (process.env.ALLOWED_USER_IDS ?? \\\"\\\").split(\\\",\\\")[0]?.trim();\\n    if (userId) {\\n      const shardDir = path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"facts_Topic\\\", userId);\\n      try {\\n        const files = await readdir(shardDir);\\n        for (const file of files) {\\n          if (!file.endsWith(\\\".md\\\")) continue;\\n          const content = await readFile(path.join(shardDir, file), \\\"utf8\\\");\\n          const lines = content.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\").filter((l) => l.startsWith(\\\"- [\\\"));\\n          for (const line of lines) {\\n            // 行格式：- [f_xxx] [ISO timestamp] text（timestamp 可省略）\\n            const m = line.match(/^- \\\\[([^\\\\]]+)\\\\](?:\\\\s+\\\\[([^\\\\]]+)\\\\])?\\\\s+(.+)$/);\\n            if (!m) continue;\\n            const fact = { id: m[1], timestamp: m[2] ?? \\\"\\\", text: m[3] };\\n            for (const sp of getSpecialists()) {\\n              if (line.toLowerCase().includes(sp.name.toLowerCase())) {\\n                if (!factsCache.data.has(sp.name)) factsCache.data.set(sp.name, []);\\n                factsCache.data.get(sp.name)!.push(fact);\\n              }\\n            }\\n          }\\n        }\\n      } catch { /* shardDir may not exist */ }\\n    }\\n  }\\n  const facts = factsCache.data.get(name) ?? [];\\n  return { totalCount: facts.length, recentFacts: facts.slice(-5).reverse() };\\n}\\n\\nexport function startStatusServer(port: number): void {\\n  if (httpServer) return;\\n\\n  const staticDir content.thinking:
The user is working on adding authentication to the status server. They've just imported `timingSafeEqual` from `node:crypto`, which is used for constant-time string comparison to prevent timing attacks when checking authentication tokens.

This is part of fixing the status server authentication issue (medium #1 from the task list).

content.thinking:
The user is implementing status server authentication. They've added an auth gate that checks for STATUS_API_TOKEN environment variable and if set, requires authentication for all /api/* routes using either Bearer token or cookie-based authentication.

This is a good security enhancement. Now I need to observe the final piece - the cookie bootstrap route for the `/?token=<token>` flow.

content.thinking:
The user is continuing to work on implementing status server authentication. They've now added cookie bootstrap functionality where visiting `/?token=xxx` will set a cookie that automatically authenticates future API requests. This is a nice zero-frontend-change approach for the Mini App.

Let me create an observation for this complete implementation.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Added optional bearer token authentication to status server API</title>
  <subtitle>STATUS_API_TOKEN env var enables auth gate for /api/* with cookie bootstrap for Mini App</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>src/status-server.ts added STATUS_API_TOKEN optional authentication protecting all /api/* routes</fact>
    <fact>Backward compatible: when STATUS_API_TOKEN unset, behavior unchanged (relies on default 127.0.0.1 bind)</fact>
    <fact>Authentication supports two methods: Authorization Bearer header for curl/scripts, or HttpOnly cookie for browser clients</fact>
    <fact>Cookie bootstrap via /?token=xxx validates token and sets 30-day HttpOnly cookie with SameSite=Lax</fact>
    <fact>tokenMatches uses timingSafeEqual for constant-time comparison preventing timing attacks</fact>
    <fact>Frontend requires zero changes: browser automatically sends cookie with same-origin fetch/SSE requests</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented optional bearer token authentication for the status server API to secure tunneled access (cloudflared/ngrok). When STATUS_API_TOKEN environment variable is set, all /api/* routes require authentication via either Authorization: Bearer header (for programmatic access) or a status_token HttpOnly cookie (for browser access). Token validation uses constant-time comparison via timingSafeEqual to prevent timing attacks. The implementation includes a cookie bootstrap mechanism: visiting /?token=xxx with a valid token sets a 30-day HttpOnly SameSite=Lax cookie, after which the browser automatically includes it in all same-origin fetch and SSE requests - requiring zero frontend changes to status-app. When STATUS_API_TOKEN is unset, all auth logic is bypassed maintaining backward compatibility with the existing localhost-only security model.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/status-server.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/status-server.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

最多跑 3 次）。\\nACP_PROMPT_RETRIES=2\\n\\n# 第一次重試前的 backoff 起始值（毫秒），每次重試翻倍：1000ms → 2000ms → 4000ms。\\n# 預設：1000。\\nACP_PROMPT_RETRY_BASE_MS=1000\\n\\n# streaming 過程中每次更新 Telegram 訊息的最小間隔（毫秒）。\\n# 愈大愈不容易被 Telegram 429 限流，但 streaming 看起來愈不流暢。\\n# 最低 500，預設 1200；如果常被 429 打到可以調到 2000–2500。\\nSTREAM_EDIT_INTERVAL_MS=1200\\n\\n# grammY autoRetry 遇到 Telegram 429 時最多願意等的秒數。\\n# Telegram 對重複觸發的 bot 會把 retry_after 階梯式拉高到 60–120s，\\n# 預設 60 夠應付大多數狀況；若 log 看到 \\\"failed 429: retry after N\\\" 且 N 在\\n# 60–120 之間才需要加大。最低 5，預設 60。\\nTG_AUTORETRY_MAX_DELAY_SECONDS=60\\n\\n# ─── Daily Intel Podcast → YouTube ──────────────────────────────────────────\\n# 預設關閉。開啟後，每份有內容的新 Daily Intel 報告會非同步建立 NotebookLM\\n# Audio Overview、轉成 MP4、以 unlisted 上傳 YouTube 並加入 Podcast playlist。\\nINTEL_PODCAST_ENABLED=0\\n\\n# 專用 NotebookLM notebook ID。建議只放 Daily Intel 報告。\\nINTEL_PODCAST_NOTEBOOK_ID=\\n\\n# NotebookLM MCP stdio server。Windows 全域安裝預設通常可直接使用下列值。\\nINTEL_PODCAST_MCP_COMMAND=notebooklm-mcp-server.cmd\\nINTEL_PODCAST_MCP_ARGS=server\\n# Media download uses ~/.notebooklm-mcp/auth.json. Run: npm run auth:notebooklm-media\\n\\n# 暫存音訊與 MP4；必須位於 MEMORY_DIR 外，成功後立即刪除，失敗最多保留 24 小時。\\nINTEL_PODCAST_TEMP_DIR=output/tmp/daily-intel-podcast\\n\\n# FFmpeg executable 與固定封面。封面建議 1280x720 PNG/JPG。\\n# 留空時使用 ffmpeg-static 與 repo 內建 assets/daily-intel/podcast-cover.png。\\nINTEL_PODCAST_FFMPEG_PATH=\\nINTEL_PODCAST_COVER_FILE=\\nINTEL_PODCAST_POLL_MS=60000\\n\\n# Google OAuth client 必須授權 https://www.googleapis.com/auth/youtube.upload\\n# 及管理 playlist 所需的 YouTube scope。影片固定先上傳為 unlisted。\\nYOUTUBE_CLIENT_ID=\\nYOUTUBE_CLIENT_SECRET=\\nYOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN=\\nYOUTUBE_PODCAST_PLAYLIST_ID=\\n\\n# Google Docs / Drive OAuth。第一次執行：\\n#   npm run google:auth -- C:\\\\path\\\\to\\\\desktop-oauth-client.json\\n# 指令只輸出授權網址，不會自動開瀏覽器；請貼到正確的 Google Profile。\\n# 授權完成後，程式會把以下三項寫入 .env；日後不需要保留 JSON 路徑。\\n# 要讓另一台 bridge 使用，可安全地在機器間複製這三個秘密環境變數，\\n# 不要貼到 Telegram 群組或提交到 Git。\\nGOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLIENT_ID=\\nGOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLIENT_SECRET=\\nGOOGLE_WORKSPACE_REFRESH_TOKEN=\\n\\n# Google Maps Platform 使用獨立 API Key，不使用上述 OAuth refresh token。\\n# 在 Cloud Console 將 Key 限制為 Places API (New)、Routes API、Geocoding API。\\nGOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY=\\n\\n\\n# /backup 指令用的 git 工作樹（會把 AIMemory + agent 設定目錄鏡像進去並推到 origin）。\\n# 留空代表停用 /backup。該資料夾必須已經是有 \\\"origin\\\" remote 的 git repo，\\n# 用以下指令 bootstrap（<repo-name> 可自選，例如 ai-memory-backup）：\\n#   mkdir <本地路徑>\\n#   cd <本地路徑>\\n#   gh repo create <repo-name> --private --source=. --remote=origin --push\\n# 省略 owner 會建到登入中的 gh 使用者名下；要建到 org 就寫 <org>/<repo-name>。\\n# 範例：BACKUP_REPO_DIR=F:\\\\AI\\\\ai-memory-backup\\nBACKUP_REPO_DIR=\\n\\n# /backup 推送的 git branch。預設 main。\\nBACKUP_BRANCH=main\\n\\n# backup repo 底下用來存 agent 設定的子目錄名。預設依 ACP_AGENT_COMMAND\\n# 自動偵測：含 \\\"kiro\\\" → kiro、含 \\\"codex\\\" → codex、含 \\\"claude\\\" → claude。\\n# 設定檔結構會長這樣：<BACKUP_REPO_DIR>/<BACKUP_AGENT_DIR_NAME>/{agents,settings,steering,powers,skills}\\n# Codex 另會備份 <AGENT_CONFIG_DIR>/config.toml 到 <BACKUP_REPO_DIR>/codex/config.toml。\\n# 不存在的 agent 子目錄會被視為 optional，安靜跳過。\\n# 只有當多台機器（Kiro + Codex）要 push 到同一個 backup repo 且想保留\\n# legacy 單一 kiro/ 樹的舊行為時，才需要手動設成 kiro。留空=自動。\\nBACKUP_AGENT_DIR_NAME=\\n\\n\\n# 每次新 session 開場時，直接內嵌進 preamble 的「最新 N 條事實」數量。\\n# 更舊的事實只列 topic 索引（agent 要時再用 list_facts MCP tool 抓）。\\n# 0 代表全部內嵌（facts 少的用戶可以這樣）。預設 30。\\nMEMORY_PREAMBLE_TAIL=10\\n\\n# preamble 要不要在「最新 N 條事實」之後附「較舊事實的 topic 索引」。\\n# 1/true/yes 開啟，其他關閉。預設開啟。\\n# 若目前 agent 沒載 MCP（例如 codex-acp）可設 0 省 token，因為 agent 也無從查 shard。\\nMEMORY_PREAMBLE_TOPICS=1\\n\\n# topics.json 最多允許幾個 topic（由 apply_topics MCP tool 在寫入時強制）。\\n# 預設 50；facts 量很大、50 個 topic 不夠用時才調高。\\n# facts-store.ts 有寫死 200 的絕對上限以防 agent 誤提超大清單。\\nMEMORY_MAX_TOPICS=50\\n\\n# /goal 的預設 turn budget — agent 回覆未帶 <<GOAL_DONE>> 時 bridge 自動塞\\n# continuation 的最大次數。達到後 goal 轉 paused，由 /goal resume 續跑。\\n# 範圍 1-50（超出自動 clamp），無效值 fallback 5。\\n# Per-goal 覆寫：`/goal 10 <objective>` 這樣在指令開頭放 1-50 的整數就會\\n# 蓋掉這個預設值；N 超範圍或非數字會回退到這裡。\\n# 小任務（寫檔/改字）留 5 夠用；大型迭代任務（修完 tsc、跑到 test 全過）\\n# 想給多一點可調到 10-20。設太高唯一風險是 agent 誤判沒完成時多燒幾輪 —\\n# 達成即 done，剩餘 budget 不會被用掉。\\n#\\n# 啟動提示：`/goal <objective>` 與 `/goal resume` 回覆會帶一個 ▶️ 開始\\n# inline button — 點一下會立刻發動第一個 continuation turn，不用再手動\\n# 打一句話。也可以照舊直接發訊息啟動 loop，效果相同。\\nGOAL_DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS=5\\n\\n# Topic 分類規則檔（見 src/topic-rules.ts）。留空時 bridge 會用\\n# <MEMORY_DIR>/topics.json（預設 F:\\\\AI\\\\AIMemory\\\\topics.json）。\\n# 檔案不存在時會從內建預設自動建立，也會被 /backup 自動收進去。\\n# 可自由編輯，bridge 大約每 2 秒重讀一次，不用 /restart。\\n# 檔案被刪或格式壞掉時會 fallback 到內建預設。\\nTOPICS_FILE=\\n\\n# ─── 群組模式 ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n# 群組中 bot 之間連續對話的最大輪數。超過後暫停回應，人類發話時重置。\\n# 防止兩個 bot 互相 mention 造成無限乒乓。預設 5。\\nGROUP_MAX_BOT_TURNS=5\\n\\n\\n# ─── Bot-to-Bot Relay（透過 Upstash Redis 中繼 + Telegram bot-to-bot） ──────\\n# 傳輸模式：\\\"redis\\\"（僅 Redis）、\\\"telegram\\\"（僅 Telegram bot-to-bot）、\\\"both\\\"（telegram 優先，redis fallback）\\nRELAY_TRANSPORT=redis\\n\\n# Telegram bot-to-bot 模式需要：對方 bot 的 @username（含 @）\\n# 雙方都需在 BotFather 啟用 \\\"Bot to Bot Communication Mode\\\"\\nRELAY_TARGET_BOT_USERNAME=\\n\\n# Upstash Redis REST API 連線資訊（免費方案：10k req/day）\\n# transport=telegram 時可留空\\nUPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL=\\nUPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN=\\n\\n# Relay 訊息同步貼到哪個群組（讓人類看到 bot 之間的對話）\\nRELAY_GROUP_CHAT_ID=\\n\\n# 本 bot 在 relay 中的顯示名稱（對方收到時會看到「來自 <name>」）\\nRELAY_BOT_NAME=\\n\\n# 空轉防治：連續幾次 relay-only turn 後停止注入（人類發話重置）\\n# RELAY_MAX_CONSECUTIVE_TURNS=10\\n\\n# Minutes to suppress new injections from the same relay peer after\\n# <<RELAY_DONE>> or RELAY_MAX_CONSECUTIVE_TURNS trips.\\n\\n\\n# ─── Local LLM (llama.cpp) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n# llama.cpp server URL。設定後 enrichment（prefilter/rerank/classify）會用本地模型。\\n# 留空代表不用本地 LLM（所有 enrichment graceful skip）。\\nLLAMACPP_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8080\\n\\n# Bridge 啟動時自動開 llama.cpp server 的腳本路徑。留空代表不自動開（需手動）。\\n# 範例：F:\\\\AI\\\\llama-cpp\\\\start-server.bat\\nLLAMACPP_START_CMD=\\n\\n# ─── Desktop Pet ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n# 啟用桌面寵物 WebSocket server（推送 agent 狀態到 pet app）。\\n# pet app 連 ws://127.0.0.1:PET_PORT 接收事件。\\nPET_ENABLED=false\\n\\n# Pet WebSocket server 監聽的 port。預設 38470。\\nPET_PORT=38470\\n\\n\\n\\n# ─── RTK (Rust Token Killer) ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n# 啟用後 preamble 會指示 agent 在 shell 指令前加 `rtk` 前綴，壓縮輸出省 token。\\n# 啟動時自動偵測 rtk binary 是否在 PATH，找不到會自動 disable。\\n# 安裝：https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk\\nRTK_SHELL_REWRITE=0\\n\\n# ─── Embedding Router ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n# 語意路由用的 embedding 模型（@xenova/transformers ONNX）。\\n# 預設 bge-small-zh-v1.5（90MB, 512維, 中文優）。\\n# 替代：Xenova/bge-m3（600MB, 1024維, 多語言）、Xenova/bge-base-zh-v1.5（110M params）\\n# 換模型後 threshold 可能要重調。\\n#EMBED_MODEL=Xenova/bge-small-zh-v1.5\\n\\n# ─── Status Web UI ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n# /status 監控 dashboard 的 HTTP server 埠號（預設 3847）。\\n#STATUS_PORT=3847\\n# 綁定介面：預設 127.0.0.1（API 會回 specialist 設定/preamble/facts，不暴露到 LAN）。\\n# Tunnel（cloudflared/ngrok）連 localhost 即可；只有要 LAN 直連才改。\\n#STATUS_BIND_HOST=127.0.0.1\\n# 有 HTTPS
